wearing old clothes, and riding horses and running through the outdoors all happy. I can’t 
remember if I was in this or not. I just got this feeling though that I would end up feeling for her 
just like Heathcliff did in the book.

4

The next day I woke up and Ma was at the kitchen table drinking her coffee. We sat there 
as usual just looking at each other with nothing to say really. I asked her what she did last night. 
Why didn’t she stay up till I got home? I tried to talk her into going for a walk. You would have 
thought I asked her to run a marathon or something. She gave me every excuse in the book. 
 “It’s so cold outside. I don’t have a nice coat to wear. Where are we going anyway?” It 
just went on and on and on. Finally, I just gave up. If Nick wants her to go outside so bad, then 
he can drag his ass over here and get her to go. 
 Of course, she probably would if Nick asked her. She listens to everything he says. He 
could ask her to swim across Lake Michigan, and she would probably try it for him. I swear 
sometimes, there’s no reason for me being born anyway, except to have somebody for Nick to 
boss around. One time, Nick told her he was getting serious about this girl he had. Like that was 
really going to happen. Anyways, Ma went so out of her way to smother this girl, it was so 
ridiculous. Having her over for steak dinners, inviting her for Thanksgiving, and trying to meet 
her parents – the whole deal. She was so deep in this shit that whe
--- PAGE 13 ---
she went haywire. She didn’t stop crying and sobbing for a week. I think she thinks she’s never 
having any grandkids. 
 The more I thought about that whole situation, the more pissed off I got, so I told Ma I 
was leaving for a little while. I would see her in a couple hours. When I got outside, I found the 
phone number Sionna gave me. I was shaking and wondering if she would even remember me at 
all. 
 “Hi.” 
“Hi, Sionna?

3 
 That day I met Sionna, when I got home, Ma was in a crazy mood. She was looking 
around the house mumbling to herself about trying to find her pain pills. She had her pills all out 
on the counter in a straight line so we didn’t have to mess with trying to figure out what to give 
her everyday. She would take a pill from the front bottle, move it to the back of the line, and so 
on. She did pretty good with it. That was one thing she did remember. That way, I didn’t have to 
be around constantly telling her what she was supposed to be taking. 
 She knew about this, but seemed confused. 
“Ma, what’s the matter?” I asked. 
“I can’t find my pain pills, I know I put them here somewhere. Did we go to church 
today? Did you see Mrs. Canasta there?” 
Okay, this was definitely weird. We hadn’t been to church for over fifteen years, and I 
aint seen Mrs. Canasta for at least the last year. 
--- PAGE 9 ---
“Ma, what are you talking about? We don’t go to church anymore. Mrs. Canasta lives 
down one level, all’s you have to do is walk down there and see her if you want. Your pills are 
right in front of you in the box for today.” 
She gave me this look I never saw before from Ma. Her eyes were all glassy and looked 
really empty. It was like she didn’t know who I was or something. She snapped out of it though, 
and said, “Oh, right, I am so sorry. I see them right here. I don’t know what got into me.” 
Wow, she was losing it. I would have to tell Nick about this. I’m sure he’ll get mad or 
something –like it was my fault. Later on I went down to Joe’s to get a beer. I had to get out of 
the apartment. Ma was freaking me out. Chances are, I would see Nick down there. He always 
came down on Mondays to watch football on T.V. Everybody would sit around, or play pool, or 
darts, and start B.Sing about crap that went on that day. It made Mondays not seem so bad after 
all. 
So I was sitting at the bar watching the Packers game – this was a big one against the 
Bears –when Nick came in. He slapped me on the back. 
“Hey, little brother. What’s shakin’?” he said. 
“I don’t know. Nothin’.” 
“What’s the matter? I can tell you’re hidin’ something. What’s goin’ on?” 
I told him the story of Ma being all confused. Like I thought, he started getting pissed off. 
“I knew she would start going crazy cooped up in that place all day” he said. “Why don’t you 
take her to the store or something? H
--- PAGE 10 ---
“Of course not. I just needed to get away from her for a while, that’s all.” He gave me 
that look like he wanted to punch me in the face. It was a little scary. I don’t think he would 
actually do it, but I’ve seen what he can do, and I don’t want that damage. He was right, of 
course, I did need to get home and watch Ma. I paid for my beer and told him I would watch it at 
home with her and I promised to get her out tomorrow and at least take a walk. 
“That’s more like it,” he said. 
God, sometimes I really hate Nick inside. I mean, he’s done so much for us, but at the 
same time, inside I really get tired of his shit. I would never tell anybody this, but I do get tired 
of it. It was freakin’ cold outside, and I walked a little faster than normal. When I looked up, I 
saw the library across the street and just halfway down the block. I should go in there, I thought. 
Who’s gonna know anyway? Ma can barely remember what the hell is going on, and Nick won’t 
know.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: In Wuthering Heights, there are all these symbols... ghosts, the moors. A love that never changes will destroy you. / It reminded me of Nick, strong and unwavering, but somehow it seemed dangerous.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (young man, thin frame, green jacket) walks out of the coffee shop onto the street, looking thoughtful and inspired, a faint smile on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We made another date to go to dinner on Friday night. As I walked to the bus stop, I thought about love that can end up killing you if you don't let it go. Maybe that was Ma's problem all along.

 “Of course I do. How are you doing?” she asked.

 “Absolutely.” She sounded so sure of herself, like she knew I was going to call at just 
that minute and had it all planned out. 
 We decided to meet downtown at a coffee shop called The Java Hut. I had seen this 
place, but never been in there. Mostly, because the people inside all looked like they were right

“Wuthering Heights.

practice being a good parent, the better the kids will be in the end. Maybe that’s why there’s so 
many shitty people in the world, cause nobody is practicing being a parent.

5 
 The sun is shining through the trees. Millions of specks of sunlight shine down on me and 
around me. The forest is thick and lush green foliage brushes against the brown mare I ride on. 
This is a deciduous forest from long ago. Moss grows thick on the north sides of the beautiful 
muscled bark on the oak and maple species. Varied woodland plants adorn the forest floor. 
Wood sorrel grows thickly under the softly treading hooves of the horse. Trillium offer up their 
creamy white and pink colors, and bleeding hearts show vibrant purples and pinks. The smell is 
intoxicating, but not as intoxicating as the smell of her neck. Her long brown hair hangs limply 
down to the small of her back. I am sitting very close behind her, holding on around her waist. 
Occasionally, I lean forward to place my face next to her neck in order to smell her distinct 
scent. It was like a mix of rose and warm grass. 
 We traveled slowly through the underbrush toward an open field. The sun there was 
uninterrupted by any growth like the forest environment we had just emerged from. It was a 
warm day. Still – with the long blades of grass drooping slightly, inviting the small flying insects 
to alight on them, only to suddenly buzz franticly the next second to a new blade. We paused in 
the center of the grassy 
--- PAGE 17 ---
rode upon. Her face is fair, heart-shaped, unlike the hundreds of everyday faces seen among our 
people. Something here stands out. I can feel such strong emotion, as if the connection between 
us is palpable electricity. The charge draws us towards each other and we embrace. I kiss her, 
and somehow I know this is the first kiss we have shared. It is the finest hour of my life. I am 
falling in love with this woman. Never have I been this vulnerable, allowing a woman to take all 
of my strength. I surrender completely and she is mine. I am drowning in her. I look into her 
eyes, and they are the lightest blue, like the early morning light. 
 “Hold!” I screamed, as I bolted upright in bed. My stomach was tight, my chest pounding 
like a drum. I was sweating, even though it was cold in my room. What the hell was that? I 
hardly ever dreamed anything. I remember it all though. There was a girl with me on the horse, a 
forest, and then we stopped in the field. I helped her off the horse and kissed her. The weird thing 
was the clothes. I really felt like I was in a movie about old kings and stuff. It was like it was my 
life for real. I knew that girl! It was Sionna, but in a different place with old clothes on. What is 
happening? Did I insert these people from life into some kind of Wuthering Heights book? That 
must have been it. The whole thing was make-believe. It had to be make-believe. Didn’t it?

--- PAGE 18 ---

of my strength. I surrender completely and she is mine. I am drowning in her. I look into her 
eyes, and they are the lightest blue, like the early morning light. 
 “Hold!” I screamed, as I bolted upright in bed. My stomach was tight, my chest pounding 
like a drum. I was sweating, even though it was cold in my room. What the hell was that? I 
hardly ever dreamed anything. I remember it all though. There was a girl with me on the horse, a 
forest, and then we stopped in the field. I helped her off the horse and kissed her. The weird thing 
was the clothes. I really felt like I was in a movie about old kings and stuff. It was like it was my 
life for real. I knew that girl! It was Sionna, but in a different place with old clothes on. What is 
happening? Did I insert these people from life into some kind of Wuthering Heights book? That 
must have been it. The whole thing was make-believe. It had to be make-believe. Didn’t it?

--- PAGE 18 ---

6

As it turns out, there are a lot of people (I mean writers and such), who have said a lot of 
things about beauty. I read this in the library one day last week, “Everything has beauty, but not 
everyone sees it.” Well, I don’t know about that. The pizza parlor aint so beautiful, and neither is 
taking care of Ma, or Nick either. Hell, Nick doesn’t have one beautiful thing about him as far as 
I’m concerned. One thing I do know though, is somebody said that beauty is only skin deep, and 
that is definitely not true.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Warm, sunny afternoon light flooding the coffee shop window, dust motes dancing in the air
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) sits across from Character_S (young woman, pale skin, short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) at 'The Java Hut'. He gestures as he recounts his dream, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I told her about my crazy dream. She just smiled and looked through me with those watery eyes. She didn't think it was crazy though.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) as she smiles and nods, her light blue eyes shimmering in the warm coffee shop light.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Did you know that Buddhists consider everything around us to be a dream?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) points gently to the wooden chair they are sitting on, tracing its texture, speaking calmly to Character_P (thin frame, green jacket).

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Well, it's not fake, it's just not something that will last for more than the present moment. That doesn't mean that it's not a chair. It just means that soon it won't be a chair. Once it was a tree, now it's a chair, and someday it will just be rust and dust.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) listens intently, leaning forward, looking completely engrossed in Character_S's words, his expression thoughtful, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Maybe you were dreaming about something from a past life, she said. Well, if that was a past life, I wish I could go back. It was so beautiful. She made me feel like I belonged somewhere; like I really mattered.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (young man, thin frame, green jacket) walks out of the coffee shop, looking up at the sky with a new perspective, feeling a deep sense of self-worth, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: If people were measured by the beauty inside of them, instead of outside, Sionna would be a ten for sure. The more you're around her, the more beautiful she becomes. I wasn't just a guy who makes pizzas, or somebody's pesky little brother.

music_intent.style_description: Thoughtful, gentle acoustic-folk with a mellow vibe, highlighting intellectual connection
music_intent.mood_arc: Inner Beauty → The Explanation → Buddhism Theme → Impermanence

scenes.setting: Day 6 - Yoga Studio (Interior)
scenes.summary: Day 6 - Yoga Studio (Interior) — She wanted me to keep going to yoga with her. I have to tell you I was happy to do it. It felt weird at first, like my body was made of metal pipes and everyone else's was made of bendable pipe cleane

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 21 ---
weird doggie position, were my shirt kept falling down over my face. I had to finally tuck the 
shirt into my pants so I could see what was going on. I thought yoga sounded pretty easy. You 
stand, you sit, you lay down - whatever. What I found out was this crap was hard. During it I felt 
completely out of breath all the time, dizzy half the time, and just plain out of shape. My groins 
felt like someone pulled me apart, just like a stretch Armstrong. My lower back was aching, and 
my shoulders hurt. I couldn't do all the push-up things, so I felt like a wimp. 
 Here's the weirdest thing of all though - after it was over, I felt so relaxed, like I just 
woke up after sleeping all night. And talk about calm, I wouldn't care if someone called me or 
Nick a big pussy right to our faces. I just wouldn't care at all. What's that about? I didn't feel 
violent, or mad, or really anything. Just calm. Not that I'm the meanest person anyway, but I 
usually would stand up for myself for cryin’ out loud. 
 "You were really great at that" Sionna said. 
 "Are you kidding me?" 
 "No, you caught on very quickly, and I have the feeling you liked it. You did like it, 
didn't you?" 
 "Um, yeah. Yeah! I did like it! I just felt so dumb. I don't know why." 
 "That's pretty common for the first few times. You'll get the hang of it, and then you'll 
understand the process", she said. 
 "What do you mean, the process?" 
 "Well, it's true what they say. Your practice is your practice. The only thin
--- PAGE 22 ---
 "That's why they call it 'practice', haha." 
 We both smiled at that one. I guess if all I'm doing is practicing, then I never have to be 
perfect. Also, I guess no one is ever "done". I had to hurry up and go home to change for work. 
It was the best night of work ever. I can't explain it, but making pizzas didn't bother me. Cleaning 
up didn't bother me. The asshole customers didn't bother me. Nothing. I was just happy to be 
there, which I don't think has ever happened to me before, unless you count payday, when I was 
just happy to be getting a check.

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that?

8

--- PAGE 23 ---

completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

scenes.setting: Day 7 - Narrator's Apartment (Living Room) - Nick's Rage
scenes.summary: Day 7 - Narrator's Apartment (Living Room) - Nick's Rage — Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all upset. Seems I haven't been around, and he's mad.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 21 ---
weird doggie position, were my shirt kept falling down over my face. I had to finally tuck the 
shirt into my pants so I could see what was going on. I thought yoga sounded pretty easy. You 
stand, you sit, you lay down - whatever. What I found out was this crap was hard. During it I felt 
completely out of breath all the time, dizzy half the time, and just plain out of shape. My groins 
felt like someone pulled me apart, just like a stretch Armstrong. My lower back was aching, and 
my shoulders hurt. I couldn't do all the push-up things, so I felt like a wimp. 
 Here's the weirdest thing of all though - after it was over, I felt so relaxed, like I just 
woke up after sleeping all night. And talk about calm, I wouldn't care if someone called me or 
Nick a big pussy right to our faces. I just wouldn't care at all. What's that about? I didn't feel 
violent, or mad, or really anything. Just calm. Not that I'm the meanest person anyway, but I 
usually would stand up for myself for cryin’ out loud. 
 "You were really great at that" Sionna said. 
 "Are you kidding me?" 
 "No, you caught on very quickly, and I have the feeling you liked it. You did like it, 
didn't you?" 
 "Um, yeah. Yeah! I did like it! I just felt so dumb. I don't know why." 
 "That's pretty common for the first few times. You'll get the hang of it, and then you'll 
understand the process", she said. 
 "What do you mean, the process?" 
 "Well, it's true what they say. Your practice is your practice. The only thin
--- PAGE 22 ---
 "That's why they call it 'practice', haha." 
 We both smiled at that one. I guess if all I'm doing is practicing, then I never have to be 
perfect. Also, I guess no one is ever "done". I had to hurry up and go home to change for work. 
It was the best night of work ever. I can't explain it, but making pizzas didn't bother me. Cleaning 
up didn't bother me. The asshole customers didn't bother me. Nothing. I was just happy to be 
there, which I don't think has ever happened to me before, unless you count payday, when I was 
just happy to be getting a check.

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that?

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

my shoulders hurt. I couldn't do all the push-up things, so I felt like a wimp. 
 Here's the weirdest thing of all though - after it was over, I felt so relaxed, like I just 
woke up after sleeping all night. And talk about calm, I wouldn't care if someone called me or 
Nick a big pussy right to our faces. I just wouldn't care at all. What's that about? I didn't feel 
violent, or mad, or really anything. Just calm. Not that I'm the meanest person anyway, but I 
usually would stand up for myself for cryin’ out loud. 
 "You were really great at that" Sionna said. 
 "Are you kidding me?" 
 "No, you caught on very quickly, and I have the feeling you liked it. You did like it, 
didn't you?" 
 "Um, yeah. Yeah! I did like it! I just felt so dumb. I don't know why." 
 "That's pretty common for the first few times. You'll get the hang of it, and then you'll 
understand the process", she said. 
 "What do you mean, the process?" 
 "Well, it's true what they say. Your practice is your practice. The only thin
--- PAGE 22 ---
 "That's why they call it 'practice', haha." 
 We both smiled at that one. I guess if all I'm doing is practicing, then I never have to be 
perfect. Also, I guess no one is ever "done". I had to hurry up and go home to change for work. 
It was the best night of work ever. I can't explain it, but making pizzas didn't bother me. Cleaning 
up didn't bother me. The asshole customers didn't bother me. Nothing. I was just happy to be 
there, which I don't think has ever happened to me before, unless you count payday, when I was 
just happy to be getting a check.

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that?

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---

scenes.summary: Day 10 - Hospital Room (Interior) - The Battle Dream — It turned out they only kept Ma in the hospital for three days. Sionna met me at the hospital for lunch one day. When I saw her, my heart did a little dance. I loved her, I just hadn't told her yet. I

source_excerpts.excerpt:
9

I ended up in a section of the library with exercise books, and there in front of me were 
lots of book on yoga, so I picked one out. Next to this I came across a section on world religion. I 
had never thought too much about religion, but my mind drifted back to a couple conversations 
Sionna and I had about Buddhist thinking, so I checked out one of those too. She wanted me to 
keep going to yoga with her. I have to tell you I was happy to do it. It felt weird at first, like my 
body was made of metal pipes and everyone else’s was made of bendable pipe cleaners, but hey, 
that’s okay. She said we all start out stiff and the more you go, the more bendable you become. 
We’ll see about that I guess. 
 I started to read the book about Buddhism. It made me stop and think. This part I read 
was about attachment, and how attaching ourselves to other people or things will make us 
miserable and cause suffering. The problem is that we are all looking for happiness, and the book 
says we look for happiness in the outside world, instead of realizing that the way we think about 
things can make us happy. People start wishing for more money and 
--- PAGE 26 ---
they want, and that leads to never-ending searching for more stuff, or “happiness”. The point is 
that all things are really one thing. It says that we are not separate from all other things – there is 
no separation to begin with, so we can end our quest for things outside of us, because things are 
all inside of us. I was trying to understand this. It said that meditating on the present moment is 
the best way to come to this “realization”. It said that Buddhism is about practicing these 
thoughts, not about just believing something, but really “experiencing” it. It said that life is 
suffering, and the cause of suffering is the search for things. Once we have things, they change, 
which leads to more suffering. It did say there was hope though. The hope was that through 
meditation and changing our minds, we can release ourselves from suffering, because we 
become enlightened and live only for the present moment. That was really cool. I never thought 
about things like this before. I had to talk to Sionna about it. 
 I called her up and told her I wished she would finally come over to my place, and even 
meet my Ma. She wasn’t really surprised, just a little hesitant. She said, “Are you sure? It’s kind 
of a big deal to meet your mother.” 
 “Hell, she probably won’t even remember meeting you when she wakes up the next 
morning. She’s so forgetful now” I responded. 
 We talked a little more. 
 “What I really need to talk to you about is some stuff I read in a book about Bud
--- PAGE 27 ---
before. I wanted to feel how I felt when I was with her – fresh, new, and looking forward to the 
future. I suddenly felt that I was growing up or something. I was starting to feel like a real 
person. I mean, it’s not like I wasn’t a real person. That would be dumb to think. But, a person 
by myself - without Nick, without Ma, without my job and apartment and belongings. It was just 
me – a person by myself. 
 “You’ve already tried it silly”, she said. 
 “What are you talking about?” 
 “You are meditating at the end of every yoga practice. You know, when we just lie there 
at the end – in Savasana, or corpse pose.” 
 She was right, of course. At the end of every yoga class, we have to just lie there and 
close our eyes.

--- PAGE 31 ---
 “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife 
I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. 
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was 
dozing off in her bed. That was the weirdest thing ever. I still felt like it was real. Why would 
Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old 
people’s brain. Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled 
with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills 
for the week in
--- PAGE 32 ---
 Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, 
explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container. “See Ma, right here is every day 
of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for 
morning and night” 
 “Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container 
suspiciously. 
 “No worries Ma. I’m goin’ to be here to make sure everything is okay, and make sure 
you get the right pill at the right time.” 
 She looked up at me with those tired brown eyes of hers. “You’re such a good boy. You 
and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?” 
 I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of 
anything. That was a laugh. The only thing Nick did lately was criticize me for anything he could 
think of. Speak of the Devil, in walked Nick. His face was the usual beet red, eyes wild with 
some kind of inner rage. His hair all helter-skelter was framing his pox-marked face. I swear 
sometimes he looked like he came straight out of prison. Like he just broke loose and was 
looking for vengeance – a kind of vengeance you see in a war movie. 
 “What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he 
laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up. 
 “Knock it off man. Somebody has to make sure she stays on track. Where have you 
been?” I sounded accusingly. 
 “Don’t start giving 
--- PAGE 33 ---
 “I know, I know, just sayin’, it’s eight o’clock at night Man. Ma’s been home for like 
four hours.” 
 “All right, look” said Nick, “I’m not giving you hell. Just what is the deal around here 
anyway? Are you going to keep her from strokin’ out, or what?” He settled himself down in the 
overstuffed brown chair, kicking his big booted feet onto the footstool. 
 “Well, that is what I’m tryin’ to do.

--- PAGE 46 ---

and
--- PAGE 47 ---

and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (young man, thin frame, white undershirt) sits in a vinyl chair in a dimly lit hospital room, dozing off. Beside him, Character_M (elderly, frail, messy brown hair, hospital gown) sleeps in a bed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It turned out they only kept Ma in the hospital for three days. Sionna met me at the hospital for lunch one day. When I saw her, my heart did a little dance. I loved her, I just hadn't told her yet. I fell asleep in the chair.

story_beats.visual_event: DREAM SEQUENCE: Character_P (young man, thin frame, wearing ancient iron armor and chainmail) stands in the mud of a chaotic medieval battlefield, surrounded by smoke and clashing warriors, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: In the dream I was a soldier of some sort. I just remember there was total chaos around me. People were yelling and screaming. Blood was on the ground.

story_beats.visual_event: DREAM SEQUENCE: Character_P (thin frame, ancient iron armor) stands over a fallen soldier in the mud. The soldier whispers something as blood bubbles from his mouth. Character_P looks confused, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Blades of shining steel were being thrust around and into other men. I felt exhausted and sad. I had just killed a man and was standing over his body. He tried to talk, but the only word was, Save her.

story_beats.visual_event: DREAM SEQUENCE OTS: Character_N (muscular, reddish-brown hair, heavy leather warrior armor) steps up tight behind Character_P (thin frame, iron armor). Camera behind P; N face in profile by P ear, wild rage, speaking. P looks away from camera.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: That you are little brother!

story_beats.visual_event: DREAM SEQUENCE: Character_N (muscular, leather armor) stabs a long, shining steel dagger into the chest of Character_P (thin frame, iron armor), sneering with intense hatred, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He suddenly stabbed the longest knife I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn't believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. I felt betrayed and so confused.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. Ma was dozing off in her bed. I still felt like it was real. Why would Nick want to kill me? It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family.
