scenes.setting: Day 12 - Narrator's Apartment (Kitchen) - The Slap
scenes.summary: Day 12 - Narrator's Apartment (Kitchen) - The Slap — I was getting Ma all set up. Sionna went with me to the drugstore and we bought a plastic container labeled with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. I explained the routine.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 31 ---
 “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife 
I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. 
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was 
dozing off in her bed. That was the weirdest thing ever. I still felt like it was real. Why would 
Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old 
people’s brain. Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled 
with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills 
for the week in
--- PAGE 32 ---
 Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, 
explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container. “See Ma, right here is every day 
of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for 
morning and night” 
 “Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container 
suspiciously. 
 “No worries Ma. I’m goin’ to be here to make sure everything is okay, and make sure 
you get the right pill at the right time.” 
 She looked up at me with those tired brown eyes of hers. “You’re such a good boy. You 
and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?” 
 I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of 
anything. That was a laugh. The only thing Nick did lately was criticize me for anything he could 
think of. Speak of the Devil, in walked Nick. His face was the usual beet red, eyes wild with 
some kind of inner rage. His hair all helter-skelter was framing his pox-marked face. I swear 
sometimes he looked like he came straight out of prison. Like he just broke loose and was 
looking for vengeance – a kind of vengeance you see in a war movie. 
 “What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he 
laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up. 
 “Knock it off man. Somebody has to make sure she stays on track. Where have you 
been?” I sounded accusingly. 
 “Don’t start giving 
--- PAGE 33 ---
 “I know, I know, just sayin’, it’s eight o’clock at night Man. Ma’s been home for like 
four hours.” 
 “All right, look” said Nick, “I’m not giving you hell. Just what is the deal around here 
anyway? Are you going to keep her from strokin’ out, or what?” He settled himself down in the 
overstuffed brown chair, kicking his big booted feet onto the footstool. 
 “Well, that is what I’m tryin’ to do.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

audio.dialogue: I got her meds all in the same spot. / Oh, holy shit! Aren't you Nurse Nancy! How did you come up with this idea anyway? / If you must know, Sionna helped me. / If you must know, if you must know! This chick sure has you wrapped around her finger.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, pizza uniform) steps close to Character_N (muscular build, dark flannel shirt), screaming with clenched fists. Character_N suddenly slaps him hard across the left cheek, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Stop it, Nick! You haven't even met her! Give her a break! / Don't you talk to me like that! / You're so hateful! What got into you? Was Ma on crack when she was pregnant with you? / Don't you EVER talk that way about MY mother, you little fuck.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, dark flannel shirt) storms out of the apartment, slamming the door. Character_P (thin frame, pizza uniform) stands alone in the kitchen, hand on his left ear, looking shocked but resolute, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My left ear was ringing. He spun around and stomped out. I had never been in a screaming fight with Nick before. It came bubbling out like a box of firecrackers. Who needs Nick? In that second, I knew I never wanted Sionna to meet that prick.

school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It

 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away

Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we

before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t

were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.”

scenes.setting: Day 12 - Kirk Street Elementary School (Flashback) - Nick's Shield
scenes.summary: Day 12 - Kirk Street Elementary School (Flashback) - Nick's Shield — Um, sorry Mrs. Stark, Nick interrupted. He didn't mean anything by it. He and Bobby were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I'll talk to him and Ma will talk to h

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 31 ---
 “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife 
I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. 
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was 
dozing off in her bed. That was the weirdest thing ever. I still felt like it was real. Why would 
Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old 
people’s brain. Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled 
with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills 
for the week in
--- PAGE 32 ---
 Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, 
explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container. “See Ma, right here is every day 
of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for 
morning and night” 
 “Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container 
suspiciously. 
 “No worries Ma. I’m goin’ to be here to make sure everything is okay, and make sure 
you get the right pill at the right time.” 
 She looked up at me with those tired brown eyes of hers. “You’re such a good boy. You 
and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?” 
 I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of 
anything. That was a laugh. The only thing Nick did lately was criticize me for anything he could 
think of. Speak of the Devil, in walked Nick. His face was the usual beet red, eyes wild with 
some kind of inner rage. His hair all helter-skelter was framing his pox-marked face. I swear 
sometimes he looked like he came straight out of prison. Like he just broke loose and was 
looking for vengeance – a kind of vengeance you see in a war movie. 
 “What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he 
laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up. 
 “Knock it off man. Somebody has to make sure she stays on track. Where have you 
been?” I sounded accusingly. 
 “Don’t start giving 
--- PAGE 33 ---
 “I know, I know, just sayin’, it’s eight o’clock at night Man. Ma’s been home for like 
four hours.” 
 “All right, look” said Nick, “I’m not giving you hell. Just what is the deal around here 
anyway? Are you going to keep her from strokin’ out, or what?” He settled himself down in the 
overstuffed brown chair, kicking his big booted feet onto the footstool. 
 “Well, that is what I’m tryin’ to do.

--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs. Stark put her glasses on, tipping her head at just the right angle so she could get a 
good head-to-toe of Nick. Actually, he might have been just an inch taller than her. “Well, Nick, 
I know you’re a good student. It’s too bad your brother here doesn’t have the same level head as 
you. It’s been such a pleasure having you at the school. We will be sorry to see you go. I guess 
I’ll let it pass this time.” Then she turned to me, “YOU, young man, need to follow your 
brother’s example and start paying attention. And you, Bobby, your mother will be getting a little 
note from me also.” She turned on her heels like a Nazi in one of those old World War II movies, 
and marched back up to the school. 
 “Nick, um thanks man. You saved my skin” I said. Bobby just looked at him and said, 
“Yeah Nick. Thanks a lot. My mom would be so upset if Mr. Pauls called her.” 
Nick just smirked and stared at us with a look of warning. 
 “You two knock it off. That is the last time I’m getting involved in your st
--- PAGE 38 ---
was like he could just go around doin’ whatever he wanted, and everything would be okay. It 
was like he was magic or something. 
 Later that night, Nick came into my room and asked how the rest of the day went. 
 “Oh, it was okay. Thanks again for standing up to Mrs. Stark for me.” 
 “So what was the joke anyway?” he asked. 
 “She was telling us we needed to learn responsibility. But it just wasn’t that, it was the 
way she said it.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

scenes.setting: Day 65 - Pizza Joint (Interior) - Reflection on the Alphas
scenes.summary: Day 65 - Pizza Joint (Interior) - Reflection on the Alphas — I found myself staring out the front window of the pizza parlor. The grass was turning green, and the weather was getting warmer. That must have been what triggered the memory of Nick and me.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t

were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away

bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we

before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs. Stark put her glasses on, tipping her head at just the right angle so she could get a 
good head-to-toe of Nick. Actually, he might have been just an inch taller than her. “Well, Nick, 
I know you’re a good student. It’s too bad your brother here doesn’t have the same level head as 
you. It’s been such a pleasure having you at the school. We will be sorry to see you go. I guess 
I’ll let it pass this time.” Then she turned to me, “YOU, young man, need to follow your 
brother’s example and start paying attention. And you, Bobby, your mother will be getting a little 
note from me also.” She turned on her heels like a Nazi in one of those old World War II movies, 
and marched back up to the school. 
 “Nick, um thanks man. You saved my skin” I said. Bobby just looked at him and said, 
“Yeah Nick. Thanks a lot. My mom would be so upset if Mr. Pauls called her.” 
Nick just smirked and stared at us with a look of warning. 
 “You two knock it off. That is the last time I’m getting involved in your st
--- PAGE 38 ---
was like he could just go around doin’ whatever he wanted, and everything would be okay. It 
was like he was magic or something. 
 Later that night, Nick came into my room and asked how the rest of the day went. 
 “Oh, it was okay. Thanks again for standing up to Mrs. Stark for me.” 
 “So what was the joke anyway?” he asked. 
 “She was telling us we needed to learn responsibility. But it just wasn’t that, it was the 
way she said it.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Warm, cozy afternoon daylight filtering into the apartment kitchen, peaceful shadows
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits cross-legged on his bedroom floor with his eyes closed, meditating peacefully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: A couple of months had gone by since my fight with Nick. He was now avoiding us like the plague. He came to see Ma once a week, but barely said anything to me. I just ignored him. Since I had started meditation, I could control where my emotions were going.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) stands in the kitchen, filling the blue weekly pill organizer with tablets. The kitchen is clean and organized, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I could see that what Nick once meant to me was silly. He was not the all-powerful beast. Nick was just a person. I am just a person. We all have inner strength. Ma was doing okay. We had her medication down to a science now.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits at the kitchen table with Character_M (elderly, frail, messy brown hair, pink housecoat) who is smiling and holding his hand, speaking warmly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma was often confused, but able to remember. The best part was, she really loved Sionna. She asked me when she was coming over, and told me how nice and polite she was. It made me happy inside.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P's face (thin frame, grey t-shirt) as he smiles with genuine happiness and self-worth, sitting in his kitchen, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: To actually feel like I was being valued for a decision that I had made – not that Nick made, but that I had made – felt awesome.

music_intent.style_description: Quiet, meditative acoustic folk with warm strings and piano, conveying emerging self-worth and calm
music_intent.mood_arc: Quiet Strength → Meditative Space → Ma's Praise → Value / Outro

scenes.setting: Day 65 - Narrator's Apartment (Living Room) - Brotherly Friendship
scenes.summary: Day 65 - Narrator's Apartment (Living Room) - Brotherly Friendship — Nick came over last night. He walked in with his big boots and brown suede jacket, threw his keys into the basket next to the front door, and bent down to kiss Ma.

before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads.

“Yeah Nick. Thanks a lot. My mom would be so upset if Mr. Pauls called her.” 
Nick just smirked and stared at us with a look of warning. 
 “You two knock it off. That is the last time I’m getting involved in your st
--- PAGE 38 ---
was like he could just go around doin’ whatever he wanted, and everything would be okay. It 
was like he was magic or something. 
 Later that night, Nick came into my room and asked how the rest of the day went. 
 “Oh, it was okay. Thanks again for standing up to Mrs. Stark for me.” 
 “So what was the joke anyway?” he asked. 
 “She was telling us we needed to learn responsibility. But it just wasn’t that, it was the 
way she said it.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs. Stark put her glasses on, tipping her head at just the right angle so she could get a 
good head-to-toe of Nick. Actually, he might have been just an inch taller than her. “Well, Nick, 
I know you’re a good student. It’s too bad your brother here doesn’t have the same level head as 
you. It’s been such a pleasure having you at the school. We will be sorry to see you go. I guess 
I’ll let it pass this time.” Then she turned to me, “YOU, young man, need to follow your 
brother’s example and start paying attention. And you, Bobby, your mother will be getting a little 
note from me also.” She turned on her heels like a Nazi in one of those old World War II movies, 
and marched back up to the school. 
 “Nick, um thanks man. You saved my skin” I said. Bobby just looked at him and said, 
“Yeah Nick. Thanks a lot. My mom would be so upset if Mr. Pauls called her.” 
Nick just smirked and stared at us with a look of warning. 
 “You two knock it off. That is the last time I’m getting involved in your st
--- PAGE 38 ---
was like he could just go around doin’ whatever he wanted, and everything would be okay. It 
was like he was magic or something. 
 Later that night, Nick came into my room and asked how the rest of the day went. 
 “Oh, it was okay. Thanks again for standing up to Mrs. Stark for me.” 
 “So what was the joke anyway?” he asked. 
 “She was telling us we needed to learn responsibility. But it just wasn’t that, it was the 
way she said it.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

scenes.summary: Day 120 - Community College Classroom (Interior) - The Return to School — My first class was a college level English class. We were going to have to write a lot, and apparently read a lot too. It had been over a decade since I was in a classroom. I thought I would be the ol

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 39 ---
 2.

I found myself staring out the front window of the pizza parlor. The grass was turning 
green, and the weather was getting warmer. That must have been what triggered the memory of 
Nick and me in grade school. I just sighed, a slight grin on my face. He had always been the 
alpha dog of the family. Well, actually, he had always been the alpha dog in any group he had 
ever been a part of. I don’t think Sionna really got the full gist of this. She hadn’t met Nick yet. 
So far, I had managed to keep them apart. But I knew sooner or later, they would end up in the 
same place. They were both the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end – of me 
anyway. What I mean is, both of them completed me in some way. Just really different ways. He 
was the leader, the supporter, the pusher, I guess. She was the soft part of my life, my comfort, 
and inner strength. Without both of them, who would I be? Who would I be? That was the 
question I had to ask myself. 
 A couple months had gone by since my fight with Nick. He was now avoiding us like the 
plague. He can over to see Ma about once a week, but barely said anything to me now. If he did, 
it was something smartass, like “how’s that girlfriend of yours Bro? Hope you’re getting 
something from her, your nose is nice and brown.” He was always saying some stupid crap like 
that. I just ignored him for the most part. Actually, Nick wasn’t bothering me that much 
anymore. Since I had started meditation, and been doing it 
--- PAGE 40 ---
control where those emotions were going. It was like being my own monitor. It was pretty useful 
really. 
 I could see that a lot of what Nick once meant to me was kind of silly, in a way. He was 
not the all-powerful beast people cracked him up to be. He was not my best friend, or my biggest 
fear. Nick was just a person. I am just a person, and on and on and on. No one is more powerful 
than I am, or less powerful than I am. I guess we all have inner strength. We just need to figure 
out how to use it. Ma was doing okay. We had her medication down to a science now, with all 
the pills laid out for each day, and for the time of day she needed them. She was often confused, 
but able to remember what happened for short periods most of the time. The best part was, she 
really loved Sionna. She asked me when she was coming over, what she was going to cook 
(when she did), and told me how “nice” and “polite” she was. It made me happy inside. To 
actually feel like I was being valued for a decision that I had made, not that Nick made, but that I 
had made, felt awesome. 
 Nick came over to visit last night. He walked in with his big boots and brown suede 
jacket, threw his keys into the basket next to the front door, and bent down to kiss Ma. She 
peeled her eyes away from the T.V. long enough to kiss his cheek and ask how his work was. 
 “Oh, you know, it was okay I guess. Those assholes next to me in the line almost caught 
me in the goddamn drill press today. I told them to pu

want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this

 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Cozy, warm light from a desk lamp in the narrator's bedroom, contrasting with quiet dark outside
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits at his desk, typing on an old desktop monitor. Character_M (elderly, pink housecoat) stands in the doorway, looking confused, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna hooked me up with this guy selling his computer, and I got him to come over and set it up in my bedroom. Ma was confused out of her mind. What's going on? What is that thing? Why do you need that?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits at his desk typing, looking exhausted but deeply focused. The clock on his wall shows 2:00 AM, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Between studying, keeping up with my pizza job, I was getting very little sleep, and when I did, I slept hard. I didn't mind it though. Every day I woke up with a purpose, different than the way I used to.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (young woman, pale skin, short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) sits beside Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) at the desk, gently pointing to a textbook and explaining, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My purpose was taking care of Ma, learning, and loving Sionna. She helped me with my homework. It was like she actually enjoyed it. Her brain was like a steel trap. I wish I could become like that.

story_beats.visual_event: DREAMY TRANSITION: Visualizing Character_P's mind: a vast, majestic library with towering wooden bookshelves. Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) walks down an aisle, placing a leather book on a shelf.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: She told me learning was like meditating. Focus on one thing, and that thing only. She told me the ancient Tibetans had a way. They pretended to see a big library in their minds. Store the fact into that book. Fascinating!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) smiles at Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) at the desk, looking happy and confident, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I started to picture my mind's library. So far, I didn't have too many books, or sections of the library, in actual use. But someday, I hoped to have a library full of facts.

music_intent.style_description: Modern lo-fi study beat with warm electric piano chords and ethereal synthesizer sweeps
music_intent.mood_arc: The Computer → Hard Work → Mental Library → Storing Facts

scenes.setting: Day 126 - City Street (Exterior) - Masters of Ourselves
scenes.summary: Day 126 - City Street (Exterior) - Masters of Ourselves — We decided to get some fresh air. The sky was blue. As we walked, some teenagers on the corner mocked us and laughed. It pissed me off inside.

I found myself staring out the front window of the pizza parlor. The grass was turning 
green, and the weather was getting warmer. That must have been what triggered the memory of 
Nick and me in grade school. I just sighed, a slight grin on my face. He had always been the 
alpha dog of the family. Well, actually, he had always been the alpha dog in any group he had 
ever been a part of. I don’t think Sionna really got the full gist of this. She hadn’t met Nick yet. 
So far, I had managed to keep them apart. But I knew sooner or later, they would end up in the 
same place. They were both the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end – of me 
anyway. What I mean is, both of them completed me in some way. Just really different ways. He 
was the leader, the supporter, the pusher, I guess. She was the soft part of my life, my comfort, 
and inner strength. Without both of them, who would I be? Who would I be? That was the 
question I had to ask myself. 
 A couple months had gone by since my fight with Nick. He was now avoiding us like the 
plague. He can over to see Ma about once a week, but barely said anything to me now. If he did, 
it was something smartass, like “how’s that girlfriend of yours Bro? Hope you’re getting 
something from her, your nose is nice and brown.” He was always saying some stupid crap like 
that. I just ignored him for the most part. Actually, Nick wasn’t bothering me that much 
anymore. Since I had started meditation, and been doing it 
--- PAGE 40 ---
control where those emotions were going. It was like being my own monitor. It was pretty useful 
really. 
 I could see that a lot of what Nick once meant to me was kind of silly, in a way. He was 
not the all-powerful beast people cracked him up to be. He was not my best friend, or my biggest 
fear. Nick was just a person. I am just a person, and on and on and on. No one is more powerful 
than I am, or less powerful than I am. I guess we all have inner strength. We just need to figure 
out how to use it. Ma was doing okay. We had her medication down to a science now, with all 
the pills laid out for each day, and for the time of day she needed them. She was often confused, 
but able to remember what happened for short periods most of the time. The best part was, she 
really loved Sionna. She asked me when she was coming over, what she was going to cook 
(when she did), and told me how “nice” and “polite” she was. It made me happy inside. To 
actually feel like I was being valued for a decision that I had made, not that Nick made, but that I 
had made, felt awesome. 
 Nick came over to visit last night. He walked in with his big boots and brown suede 
jacket, threw his keys into the basket next to the front door, and bent down to kiss Ma. She 
peeled her eyes away from the T.V. long enough to kiss his cheek and ask how his work was. 
 “Oh, you know, it was okay I guess. Those assholes next to me in the line almost caught 
me in the goddamn drill press today. I told them to pu
--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that?

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?”

sudden, he just

my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to

wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment.

 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you

audio.dialogue: Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I'm going to need a new one! I started laughing.
