scenes.setting: Day 130 - Nick's Apartment (Living Room) - Algebra to a Bug
scenes.summary: Day 130 - Nick's Apartment (Living Room) - Algebra to a Bug — Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke sputtering out. At that moment I wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

I found myself staring out the front window of the pizza parlor. The grass was turning 
green, and the weather was getting warmer. That must have been what triggered the memory of 
Nick and me in grade school. I just sighed, a slight grin on my face. He had always been the 
alpha dog of the family. Well, actually, he had always been the alpha dog in any group he had 
ever been a part of. I don’t think Sionna really got the full gist of this. She hadn’t met Nick yet. 
So far, I had managed to keep them apart. But I knew sooner or later, they would end up in the 
same place. They were both the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end – of me 
anyway. What I mean is, both of them completed me in some way. Just really different ways. He 
was the leader, the supporter, the pusher, I guess. She was the soft part of my life, my comfort, 
and inner strength. Without both of them, who would I be? Who would I be? That was the 
question I had to ask myself. 
 A couple months had gone by since my fight with Nick. He was now avoiding us like the 
plague. He can over to see Ma about once a week, but barely said anything to me now. If he did, 
it was something smartass, like “how’s that girlfriend of yours Bro? Hope you’re getting 
something from her, your nose is nice and brown.” He was always saying some stupid crap like 
that. I just ignored him for the most part. Actually, Nick wasn’t bothering me that much 
anymore. Since I had started meditation, and been doing it 
--- PAGE 40 ---
control where those emotions were going. It was like being my own monitor. It was pretty useful 
really. 
 I could see that a lot of what Nick once meant to me was kind of silly, in a way. He was 
not the all-powerful beast people cracked him up to be. He was not my best friend, or my biggest 
fear. Nick was just a person. I am just a person, and on and on and on. No one is more powerful 
than I am, or less powerful than I am. I guess we all have inner strength. We just need to figure 
out how to use it. Ma was doing okay. We had her medication down to a science now, with all 
the pills laid out for each day, and for the time of day she needed them. She was often confused, 
but able to remember what happened for short periods most of the time. The best part was, she 
really loved Sionna. She asked me when she was coming over, what she was going to cook 
(when she did), and told me how “nice” and “polite” she was. It made me happy inside. To 
actually feel like I was being valued for a decision that I had made, not that Nick made, but that I 
had made, felt awesome. 
 Nick came over to visit last night. He walked in with his big boots and brown suede 
jacket, threw his keys into the basket next to the front door, and bent down to kiss Ma. She 
peeled her eyes away from the T.V. long enough to kiss his cheek and ask how his work was. 
 “Oh, you know, it was okay I guess. Those assholes next to me in the line almost caught 
me in the goddamn drill press today. I told them to pu
--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something.

story_beats.visual_event: Bob (crude young man, stubble, backwards baseball cap) holds a ceramic pipe, blowing a thick cloud of smoke. Beside him, Sammy (stoned young man, scruffy beard) drinks from a beer can, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Come on, Bro. You haven't been here in ages. / This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, gets hammered, and tells his stories. Boring. Bob and Sammy were complaining about their girlfriends.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, flannel shirt) laughs loudly, pointing a finger at Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) as Bob and Sammy grin mockingly.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Hey, unless you know some Victoria's Secret models, there ain't many good-looking girls in Milwaukee, period. Ain't that right, Bro? Oh wait, yeah, never mind. He's got the perfect girlfriend. She has him wrapped.

story_beats.visual_event: Sammy (scruffy beard) slaps his leg, laughing mockingly at Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie), who leans back calmly in the rocking chair, a quiet smirk on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Oh, wow, dude! Is that right? Do you need a 'get out of jail free' card? / The three of them kept it up, while I just leaned back and smiled. What a bunch of morons.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) speaks back calmly but sharply. Character_N (muscular, flannel shirt) and his friends stare in surprise.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You three wouldn't recognize class and intelligence if it jumped up and bit your ass. Tyra Banks could ask you for her number, and you wouldn't recognize the opportunity.

story_beats.visual_event: Sammy (scruffy beard) bursts into a loud, stoned laugh, raising his hands. Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) shakes his head, realizing the intellectual divide, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Tyra Banks can move in, take over my bank account, and have my fucking car! / Tonight was not the night for intelligent talk. Explaining Sionna to them would be like explaining algebra to a bug. Useless.

story_beats.visual_event: WIDE SHOT of Joe's Bar, packed with working-class patrons, smoke filling the air. Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, dark grey hoodie) stands near the back, observing, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We went into Joe's Bar. The place was absolutely packed. Men in dirty factory jackets and heavy work boots stood elbow-to-elbow, slamming down their beer bottles. Rock music was blasting.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) looks around the smoky bar at people laughing, shouting, and playing pool. His expression is distant, as if observing another species, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: This was the place where people like us came. By people like us, I mean the working poor. People who worked their asses off forty hours for a measly paycheck, then spent a third of it drinking.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P's face (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) in the dim neon lighting, showing a quiet, analytical expression, shaking his head slightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It seemed strange, as if I were an alien visiting the planet, amused by the antics of the population. Everyone appeared familiar, but the lifestyle was starting to confuse me. It felt so – dumb. Just dumb.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) steps up to the bar counter beside Character_N (older brother, muscular build, dark flannel shirt) as patrons move aside respectfully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: These people felt dumb, like they were numb from the world. They enjoyed being miserable five days, happy for one, and regretful on Sunday. Oh well, when in Rome, do as the Romans do.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, flannel shirt) clinks a glass of Jack and Coke with Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie), who takes a sip of a Budweiser bottle, forcing a slight smile, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stepped up beside Nick, who everyone scooched over for. Nick ordered a Jack and Coke, Sammy and Bob had PBRs, and I had a Bud.

music_intent.style_description: Loud, driving distorted bar-rock music mixed with a slightly eerie, detached synthesizer drone
music_intent.mood_arc: The Clatter → Alien Sensation → Numb World → The Bar Counter

scenes.setting: Night 130 - Joe's Bar - Pool Room (Interior) - Old Flame, New Spark
scenes.summary: Night 130 - Joe's Bar - Pool Room (Interior) - Old Flame, New Spark — Hold on a second. That looks like Lindsey over there. She was his ex-girlfriend. He had dumped her like a sack of potatoes because she was getting too into his personal shit.

master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

audio.dialogue: Hold on a second. That looks like Lindsey over there. She was his ex-girlfriend. He had dumped her like a sack of potatoes because she was getting too into his personal shit.

story_beats.visual_event: Lindsey (pretty young woman, light blonde hair, brown eyes, pink sweater, boots) stands near a pool table. A protective mystery man with black wavy hair, dark eyes, and thick caterpillar eyebrows stands beside her, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Yep, that looks like her, Sammy said. Who's she with? Who's that asshole? Nick said. Why does it matter Bro? You broke up with her, remember? I gave him a questioning look.

audio.dialogue: I just keep tabs on her. You know what I mean? No one told me she was seeing anyone. He started to get that look of craziness in his face.

audio.dialogue: Drop it man. Don't worry about it. It didn't work out. She can see whoever she wants. I had to state her case because I knew that crazy look. I really didn't want a bar fight.

audio.dialogue: Kiss my ass! Did I ask your opinion?

“Do you have a library card?” she asked. 
“No, how do I get one of those?” 
She showed me this piece of paper and I wrote down my name and address. Then she 
gave me a “temporary” paper card. She said a plastic one would be mailed to me within the 
week. That was cool, cause I got the mail anyway. It was like I could get anything in here for 
free. Who needed school anyway when you could get the same thing for free in the library? What 
stupid shit spends his money on classes when everything is right here, in this building?

I had no idea what I wanted in this place, but I just started walking down the rows 
looking at all the books. For some reason I ended up in the “classic literature” area and saw all 
these books by Shakespeare, Dickens, Eliot, Dumas, and Wilde. There was so many I didn’t 
know which one to pick out, but I thought they were all important if they were in that section, so 
I just picked out “Wuthering Heights” by a lady named Emily Bronte. I checked out the book 
with the lady at the desk, who gave me a thin little smile, and then walked home in the cold. 
Shit, I missed the whole game. Just as well, Chicago won by a touchdown anyway. Ma 
was already in bed when I got there. Since there was nothing to do, I started reading that book. It 
was pretty weird at first, because it was about a place in England a long time ago. It was about a 
guy named Heathcliff and a girl named Catherine. So far, it was a little like a soap opera. There 
was a lot of people 
--- PAGE 12 ---
wearing old clothes, and riding horses and running through the outdoors all happy. I can’t 
remember if I was in this or not. I just got this feeling though that I would end up feeling for her 
just like Heathcliff did in the book.

4

The next day I woke up and Ma was at the kitchen table drinking her coffee. We sat there 
as usual just looking at each other with nothing to say really. I asked her what she did last night. 
Why didn’t she stay up till I got home? I tried to talk her into going for a walk. You would have 
thought I asked her to run a marathon or something. She gave me every excuse in the book. 
 “It’s so cold outside. I don’t have a nice coat to wear. Where are we going anyway?” It 
just went on and on and on. Finally, I just gave up. If Nick wants her to go outside so bad, then 
he can drag his ass over here and get her to go. 
 Of course, she probably would if Nick asked her. She listens to everything he says. He 
could ask her to swim across Lake Michigan, and she would probably try it for him. I swear 
sometimes, there’s no reason for me being born anyway, except to have somebody for Nick to 
boss around. One time, Nick told her he was getting serious about this girl he had. Like that was 
really going to happen. Anyways, Ma went so out of her way to smother this girl, it was so 
ridiculous. Having her over for steak dinners, inviting her for Thanksgiving, and trying to meet 
her parents – the whole deal. She was so deep in this shit that whe
--- PAGE 13 ---
she went haywire. She didn’t stop crying and sobbing for a week. I think she thinks she’s never 
having any grandkids. 
 The more I thought about that whole situation, the more pissed off I got, so I told Ma I 
was leaving for a little while. I would see her in a couple hours. When I got outside, I found the 
phone number Sionna gave me. I was shaking and wondering if she would even remember me at 
all. 
 “Hi.” 
“Hi, Sionna?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, flannel) walks toward Lindsey (pink sweater, blonde), facing her. Boyfriend (black wavy hair, caterpillar eyebrows) steps between them facing Nick protectively, Lindsey behind him. Nick faces the couple, not the camera.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: What's up Lindsey?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) tries to step between Character_N (muscular build) and the boyfriend. Character_N pushes Character_P aside roughly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Where did you meet this one? Nick sneered. Nick, leave it alone! I tried to step in front of him, but he pushed me to the side. What the fuck is wrong with you? the boyfriend said. You got a problem with me?

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_N's flaring nostrils and clenched fists, red anger in his eyes. In the background, Lindsey presses her back against the pool room wall, terrified, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You're in my face like a little bitch! the boyfriend yelled. Uh oh, that did it. Red fire came out of Nick's eyes. Nostrils flared, fists clenched. This had the feel of an all-out brawl.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) steps in front of Character_N (muscular build), looking him pleadingly in the eye. Character_N points a menacing finger at Character_P's face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stepped up closer than I ever dared. Nick, listen – you don't have a problem with this guy. Lindsey can see whoever she wants. Leave it. Keep out of it! Nick screamed. You're next if you don't get out of my face!

story_beats.visual_event: SLOW MOTION: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) yells in Character_N's face. Character_N's right fist draws back and punches toward Character_P's face. Character_P ducks, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Stop it! Stop it! You're crazy! No one cares about your ego Nick! I was terrified of being hit, but also of being alone, without Nick to back me up. Time stood still. Nick's fist drew back and flew toward my face.

story_beats.visual_event: Joe (bar owner) and a large bouncer tackle Character_N (muscular build). Character_N head-butts the bouncer. Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) turns and runs out the door, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I swerved back, catching my balance. Joe and his bouncer tackled Nick. Lindsey grabbed her boyfriend, pushing him out. Nick head-butted the bouncer to escape. Bob and Sammy yelled. I ran.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey hoodie) sprints at high speed down a dark, cold city sidewalk, leaping over trash cans and dodging other pedestrians, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was running like a bear was chasing me, jumping over obstacles in the street, until my chest felt about to explode. I thought I was running home, but I was running to Sionna's house.

music_intent.mood_arc: AC-DC Vibe → Rage and Defiance → The Warning → Slow-Motion Punch

scenes.setting: Night 130 / Day 131 - Sionna's House / Bedroom (Interior) - Bravely Compassionate
scenes.summary: Night 130 / Day 131 - Sionna's House / Bedroom (Interior) - Bravely Compassionate — I ran into the arms of the only person that really understood me, the only person I felt I could trust. I told Sionna the whole story. She just stared at me, taking her time to think.

a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.”

your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.”

yet, but might become like Buddha or something?”

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, grey t-shirt) lies down on a green suede sofa, resting his head in the lap of Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt), lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna sat down on her green suede sofa, and I lied down next to her. I rested my head in her lap, and she started to massage my head. It felt like such a relief. Sionna, I said, I feel so comfortable. It's like I have finally come home.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (pale skin, short brown hair, light blue eyes) as a watery film covers her eyes, a tear rolling down her cheek as she leans down to kiss Character_P's forehead.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Me too honey. Me too. / The purpose of your life is to find the true self within you, and to honor it. To be the best person you can while you're here. That's how I see it.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) smiles sleepily, looking up at Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt) as she gently brushes his hair with her fingers, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I hope I can, I whispered. My eyes started to close. I had been through the full range of emotions today, from scared shitless to giddy. It was totally messed up, but in the end, it was epic.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) closes his eyes, breathing deeply and peacefully on the couch. Character_S (short brown hair) smiles and gently drapes a soft blanket over him, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I knew nothing else from this day. I drifted off to sleep, feeling completely safe for the first time in my life.

music_intent.mood_arc: Relief → Like Coming Home → Finding True Self → Drifting Off / Outro

scenes.setting: Night 130 - Dreamscape - Lake of Serenity (Exterior) - Water and Fire
scenes.summary: Night 130 - Dreamscape - Lake of Serenity (Exterior) - Water and Fire — I had a dream. The world was in black and white. I felt exhausted, as if I had just survived a three-day battle. My boots were muddy, my tunic stained. I walked to a lake to drink.

practice being a good parent, the better the kids will be in the end. Maybe that’s why there’s so 
many shitty people in the world, cause nobody is practicing being a parent.

5 
 The sun is shining through the trees. Millions of specks of sunlight shine down on me and 
around me. The forest is thick and lush green foliage brushes against the brown mare I ride on. 
This is a deciduous forest from long ago. Moss grows thick on the north sides of the beautiful 
muscled bark on the oak and maple species. Varied woodland plants adorn the forest floor. 
Wood sorrel grows thickly under the softly treading hooves of the horse. Trillium offer up their 
creamy white and pink colors, and bleeding hearts show vibrant purples and pinks. The smell is 
intoxicating, but not as intoxicating as the smell of her neck. Her long brown hair hangs limply 
down to the small of her back. I am sitting very close behind her, holding on around her waist. 
Occasionally, I lean forward to place my face next to her neck in order to smell her distinct 
scent. It was like a mix of rose and warm grass. 
 We traveled slowly through the underbrush toward an open field. The sun there was 
uninterrupted by any growth like the forest environment we had just emerged from. It was a 
warm day. Still – with the long blades of grass drooping slightly, inviting the small flying insects 
to alight on them, only to suddenly buzz franticly the next second to a new blade. We paused in 
the center of the grassy 
--- PAGE 17 ---
rode upon. Her face is fair, heart-shaped, unlike the hundreds of everyday faces seen among our 
people. Something here stands out. I can feel such strong emotion, as if the connection between 
us is palpable electricity. The charge draws us towards each other and we embrace. I kiss her, 
and somehow I know this is the first kiss we have shared. It is the finest hour of my life. I am 
falling in love with this woman. Never have I been this vulnerable, allowing a woman to take all 
of my strength. I surrender completely and she is mine. I am drowning in her. I look into her 
eyes, and they are the lightest blue, like the early morning light. 
 “Hold!” I screamed, as I bolted upright in bed. My stomach was tight, my chest pounding 
like a drum. I was sweating, even though it was cold in my room. What the hell was that? I 
hardly ever dreamed anything. I remember it all though. There was a girl with me on the horse, a 
forest, and then we stopped in the field. I helped her off the horse and kissed her. The weird thing 
was the clothes. I really felt like I was in a movie about old kings and stuff. It was like it was my 
life for real. I knew that girl! It was Sionna, but in a different place with old clothes on. What is 
happening? Did I insert these people from life into some kind of Wuthering Heights book? That 
must have been it. The whole thing was make-believe. It had to be make-believe. Didn’t it?

--- PAGE 18 ---

6

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: SURREAL DREAM LIGHT: Starts as a stark, low-contrast black-and-white landscape, then explodes into incredibly vibrant, saturated technicolor
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: BLACK-AND-WHITE: Character_P (as a medieval warrior, dirty leather boots, mud-stained tunic) walks tiredly toward a quiet lake, leaning down to drink water from his cupped hands, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had a dream. The world was in black and white. I felt exhausted, as if I had just survived a three-day battle. My boots were muddy, my tunic stained. I walked to a lake to drink.

story_beats.visual_event: SURREAL TRANSITION TO COLOR: Character_S (with long blowing brown hair, glowing light blue eyes, a flowing white angel gown) rises gracefully from the rippling center of the lake, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: From the middle of the lake, she rose. It was a slow-motion dance with wind and water swirling around her. She had long brown hair, and eyes of light blue, like an angel in a blowing white gown.

story_beats.visual_event: The world bursts into saturated colors. Character_P (medieval tunic) and Character_S (white angel gown) embrace lovingly at the edge of the clear blue water, bathed in bright sunshine, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The world suddenly had color! Vibrant greens, blues, and yellows shocked me. We embraced, and a feeling of total, complete serenity enveloped me. Then, without warning, alarm was there.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (appearing as a giant, eyes of fire, large teeth) emerges from the dark woods, swinging a massive metal mace on a clanging iron chain, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick emerged from the woods. He appeared as a giant with eyes of fire and large teeth, whirling a heavy mace on a clanging chain above his head. He was out for us, and I tried to step in front of her.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (white angel gown) spreads her arms wide. An invisible, glowing blue water-shield pushes the giant Character_N (eyes of fire) back, causing him to shrink, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She spread her arms, her white sleeves blowing like wings. An invisible force pushed Nick back. Good against evil – water against fire. Nick's face was wild because his weapon failed him. He shrank to normal size.

story_beats.visual_event: The wind stops. Character_N fades into thin air. Character_S (long brown hair, white gown) turns and smiles warmly at Character_P. He tenderly strokes her hair and kisses her, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick disappeared into thin air. She turned and smiled at me. I felt total relief. I started to stroke her real hair and kiss her face, suddenly becoming aware of the taste of her real lips...

music_intent.style_description: Mournful solo flute in black and white, transitioning to a massive orchestral romantic swell, then crashing into heavy-metal distortion before resolving to peaceful harp
music_intent.mood_arc: Desolation (B&W) → The Lady of the Lake → Serene Embrace → Giant of Fire

scenes.setting: Night 130 / Day 131 - Sionna's House - Bedroom (Interior) - A New Morning
scenes.summary: Night 130 / Day 131 - Sionna's House - Bedroom (Interior) - A New Morning — I bolted upright! What the? I said, confused. Oh my gosh, Sionna rubbed her eyes. We must have drifted off. We were folded together on her couch, disoriented. The kitchen clock showed three a.m. I tol

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, blue t-shirt) sits on his bed, looking at his High School diploma, smiling thoughtfully, a tear of relief and pride almost forming in his eyes, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had graduated from high school with average grades, figuring I'd just get a regular job. But now, I constantly had to re-evaluate who I once was, and who I was now. I could see myself growing. I felt like crying from pure growth.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (silver hair, pink housecoat) sits in her armchair, looking confusedly at a framed photo of Nick. Character_P (thin frame, blue t-shirt) sits on the armrest, comforting her, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick was completely avoiding us. Ma was confused, wondering why he never came around. She started to see me as the leader of our little family. I covered for him, telling her he had poker games or was at the gym.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, yellow floral shirt) smiles warmly, serving a delicious baking dish of golden-brown casserole to Character_M (pink housecoat, afghan blanket) in the dining room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna came over quite a bit. Ma was happy to have her around. Sionna was so kind and made Ma feel special. She hooked Ma completely when she cooked this outrageous butternut squash, cheese, and crunchy nut casserole.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, yellow shirt) patiently reads from a book to Character_M (pink housecoat, silver hair), who folds her hands in her lap, listening peacefully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She helped me organize Ma's pill containers correctly, which was good because I was so tired I could have made mistakes. She even read to Ma. Within an hour, Ma would forget everything, but she remembered the feeling behind it.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, blue t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair) sit together on the living room sofa, leaning their heads together, completely exhausted but smiling blissfully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Most of the time, we would get Ma to bed, come back out to the living room or my room, start talking, maybe have sex if we were lucky, then fall into a coma. We were so happy.

music_intent.style_description: Steady acoustic guitar and light hand percussion groove with warm electric piano, a quiet violin, and happy glockenspiel bells
music_intent.mood_arc: The Passing Months → Intellectual Growth → Brother's Absence → Sionna's Care

scenes.setting: Day 210 - Philosophy Classroom (Interior) - Thinking About Thinking
scenes.summary: Day 210 - Philosophy Classroom (Interior) - Thinking About Thinking — I was into my first philosophy class, which seemed to help me manage things. The teacher, a middle-aged guy with a beer gut, told us that philosophy was the science of 'thinking about thinking'. A par

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Slightly clinical fluorescent classroom lighting, dusty chalk particles caught in sunbeams from windows
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: An older professor with a beer gut paces before a green chalkboard. Character_P (young man, thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits at his wooden desk, taking notes, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was into my first philosophy class, which seemed to help me manage things. The teacher, a middle-aged guy with a beer gut, told us that philosophy was the science of 'thinking about thinking'. A paradox.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on the chalkboard as the professor writes: 'Socrates: The unexamined life is not worth living.' Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) stares at the quote, deeply absorbed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Socrates said, 'The unexamined life is not worth living.' I was starting to believe this. What was the purpose of my life? It certainly wasn't working in a pizza parlor, or just taking care of Ma, though these were parts.

story_beats.visual_event: MONTAGE: Quick cuts of Mitch handing out towels at the gym, Joe standing behind the bar, and Character_N (older brother, muscular build) looking intimidating, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked around at all the people in my world: Joe, the bar owner; Mitch at Moxie's; or even Nick. Did these people examine their lives? I knew the answer was 'no'. But did that mean their lives weren't worth living?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) walks down a leafy college campus sidewalk, a textbook tucked under his arm, looking thoughtful and slightly exhausted, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: There were so many questions that I couldn't answer, and as far as I could tell, no one in the books could answer them either. The more tired I got, the more confused I got.

music_intent.style_description: Thoughtful, slow electric piano chords with a low ambient synth bed, gentle solo cello, and delicate acoustic guitar picking
music_intent.mood_arc: The Classroom → Unexamined Life → A Baby's Growth → Paradox / Outro
