Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Warm daylight from the bedroom window, cozy study atmosphere
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, dark blue t-shirt, jeans) sits at his desk typing, looking frustrated as he stares at the computer monitor, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was writing a little paper on Rene Descartes, the founder of modern philosophy. He was known to say, 'I think, therefore I am.' We were talking in class about dualism – the mind versus the body. I was floundering.

story_beats.visual_event: The doorbell rings. Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) opens the front door. Character_S (short brown hair pinned up regally, flowered shirt, sandals) stands in the doorway, smiling beautifully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then the doorbell rang. I hadn't expected to see Sionna for another couple of hours, but there she stood in all her beauty. How could I separate her mind and her body? They were a force to be dealt with.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair pinned up regally, flowered shirt) flops down onto Character_P's bed with an exhausted sigh, stretching out. Character_P sits beside her, watching her adore-lingly.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Geez, what a day! I've been running around like a chicken with its head cut off. Yoga, store, class notes on comparative religion, library, and then here.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) smiles warmly. Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) smiles and squints her light blue eyes playfully at him, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I love you, I said. Have you been listening to anything I said? she smiled and squinted. Every word, I answered. I took her in like it would be the last day of my life, making a memory.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) playfully jumps on the bed, kissing Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) as they roll around giggling, having fun, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My conscious mind was definitely influencing my body, looking at the excitement I had created inside my jeans! She started laughing, and I jumped on her to kiss and roll around. I needed a break anyway.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) lie side-by-side on the bed, looking up at the ceiling together, smiling.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: So what's your paper about? / Oh, thoughts on dualism. / Perception is so cool to think about. / Like, is this bed here when you don't see it?

music_intent.style_description: Thoughtful, cozy acoustic guitar picking with delicate flute and happy hand-claps, resolving to warm electric piano
music_intent.mood_arc: Dualism Paper → Doorbell / Sionna Arrives → Regal Beauty → Playful Kisses

scenes.setting: Day 215 - Narrator's Bedroom (Interior) - Conceived at Woodstock
scenes.summary: Day 215 - Narrator's Bedroom (Interior) - Conceived at Woodstock — You know my parents were hippies, right? / Well, I was born in 1970, and my mom always said she got pregnant with me at Woodstock.

symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Warm afternoon light turning into a soft amber sunset glow outside the bedroom window
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair pinned up, flowered shirt, light blue eyes) sits up on the bed, looking at Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) with a warm, nostalgic smile.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: You know my parents were hippies, right? / Well, I was born in 1970, and my mom always said she got pregnant with me at Woodstock.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) gestures with her hands as she explains. Character_P (thin frame) listens, fascinated, gently playing with the ends of her hair.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: My Dad went by 'Mushroom' and my Mom went by 'Ginger'. How do you think I got the name Sionna? It's a Celtic Goddess, matron of the River Shannon, the possessor of wisdom from the Celtic underworld. Fascinating, huh?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) looks thoughtful, thinking of her parents. Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) kisses her forehead gently.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: My mom and dad smoked a lot of pot and dropped a lot of acid. I'm surprised my brain works at all. I was an only child because they were too interested in their extracurricular activities to worry about diapers. It was hard keeping friends.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) smiles. Character_S (short brown hair) smiles back, her head cocked to the side as she listens, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Everyone knew my Ma because she worked for the schools, so I couldn't be a fuck-up. Though Nick seemed to get away with everything. He's a master of deception. He intimidates everyone. / Nick sounds interesting. I need to meet him.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) smiles, playing with the edge of her collar. Character_S (short brown hair, flowered shirt) chuckles warmly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Trust me, take your time. It'll just make you feel inferior. Where are your parents now? / Where all old hippies go – the farm. They're in Door County on ten acres in a dilapidated farmhouse, growing their own food.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P (thin frame, dark blue t-shirt) with a peaceful, slightly envious expression, staring back at the ceiling, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Wish I could see that. / You're perfect. / You're ridiculous! Have you ever done drugs? / I breathed secondhand smoke before age three. / I was under Nick's watch, so I never could experiment.

music_intent.style_description: Nostalgic 1960s acoustic picking with a gentle slide-guitar, Celtic harp, a slow earthy violin, and deep cello
music_intent.mood_arc: Hippie Roots → Mushroom and Jasmine → River of Knowledge → The Door County Farm

scenes.setting: Night 215 - City Street / Outside Pizza Shop (Exterior) - Hottest Topic in Town
scenes.summary: Night 215 - City Street / Outside Pizza Shop (Exterior) - Hottest Topic in Town — I got a large black coffee to go. Walking back, I saw Kevin McCleary, a regular weight lifter from Moxie's Gym. Hey man! Kevin smiled excitedly. Haven't seen you much at the gym. What's going on?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, red pizza shop polo, black apron) stands outside holding a paper cup of coffee. He looks up as Kevin McCleary (muscular, short gelled hair parted crisply, tight gym shirt) walks up, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I got a large black coffee to go. Walking back, I saw Kevin McCleary, a regular weight lifter from Moxie's Gym. Hey man! Kevin smiled excitedly. Haven't seen you much at the gym. What's going on?

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Kevin McCleary's face (short gelled hair, tight shirt) as he gesticulates with huge muscular arms, eyes wide with excitement.

audio.speaker: Kevin McCleary
audio.dialogue: Man, I heard what happened at Joe's! What the hell? / I heard you stopped Nick from beating some guy's ass! Man, that's tough shit! They said you hauled off and clocked Nick!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, red polo shirt) looks terrified, shaking his head and waving his hands in a frantic attempt to correct Kevin, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: They said you had some pretty big balls! Kevin said. Look, I don't know who told you that, but it totally isn't the truth! I said. Nick almost kicked my ass. Don't think I'm a hero or anything. Please tell everyone it wasn't like that.

story_beats.visual_event: Kevin McCleary (muscular, tight shirt) chuckles and pats Character_P (thin frame, red polo) on the back. Character_P looks deeply uneasy, clutching his coffee cup tightly.

audio.speaker: Kevin McCleary
audio.dialogue: Sure, sure man, I got you covered. / But I'm just saying, it's all over town. You're the hottest topic flying around! People are saying Nick's little brother is a badass!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, red polo shirt) walks back into the pizza shop door, his head down, looking anxious and sick to his stomach, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: That was unreal. Why do people give a shit? It was like the old telephone game. It made the pit of my stomach ache. I would be glad to be a hero, just not in a story involving Nick. I felt like a fugitive.

music_intent.style_description: Slow, moody electric guitar transitioning into fast, bouncy acoustic strums and a tense, low droning cello
music_intent.mood_arc: The Break → Gym Gossip → Rumor Mill → Fugitive Stomach

scenes.setting: Night 215 - Narrator's Bathroom (Interior) - Facing the Mirror
scenes.summary: Night 215 - Narrator's Bathroom (Interior) - Facing the Mirror — I called Sionna and told her. She said I should look at it as an opportunity for Nick to eat some humble pie, and accept being the hero for once. As I brushed my teeth, I heard her voice in my head.

Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Slightly harsh, white bathroom mirror lighting, highlighting the contours of the narrator's face
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, white sleeveless undershirt) stands before his bathroom mirror, brushing his teeth, staring seriously at his reflection, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I called Sionna and told her. She said I should look at it as an opportunity for Nick to eat some humble pie, and accept being the hero for once. As I brushed my teeth, I heard her voice in my head.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P's face in the mirror (thin frame) as he spits, rinses his mouth, and stares deeply at himself, a look of growing strength and resolve on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had to face the truth – I wasn't the same shadow of the boy I once was. I had to grow some balls. Why did I shy away from the spotlight? Why should Nick have all the glory, and me have none?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, white undershirt) turns off the bathroom light, plunging the room into shadow as he exits, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Life was so mixed up. As I fell asleep, I came to the conclusion that it's over. I will have to deal with the consequences from Nick someday. I just hoped that someday would never come.

music_intent.style_description: Quiet, introspective solo electric piano and a soft, beautiful violin note resolving warmly
music_intent.mood_arc: Staring Back → Sionna's Wisdom → Out of the Shadow / Outro

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) walks up to a grey duplex with blue shutters, hugging Character_S (short brown hair, cozy purple oversized sweater) tightly on the front porch, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The next day after class, we planned our big afternoon with the weed she had acquired. I saw her sitting on her front porch. It was an old, grey neighborhood. She smiled, and I hugged her a little too long before we went inside.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) and Character_S (purple oversized sweater) sit together on a lumpy, chocolate-colored living room couch.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Well, I got a little bit of weed for us to smoke. / The guy I know on campus sold me some because I've known him for four years.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater) opens a small red wooden box on her coffee table, retrieving a four-inch blue ceramic pipe with black swirly designs, smiling proudly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: What do we smoke it in? I asked. I have an old ceramic bowl. She rummaged around, opened a little red oriental box, and retrieved a small four-inch blue ceramic pipe with black swirly designs. Where did you get that? I asked.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater) and Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) laugh together on the couch.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Oh my gosh, I've had this for ten or twelve years! / I really don't sit around and smoke pot – I've been a little busy. / Well, that's what we're doing today!

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (purple sweater, light blue eyes) as she lights the blue ceramic pipe with a lighter, inhaling deeply and then coughing, blowing out a thick, fragrant cloud of smoke.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: I am glad I get to do this, because I love just being with you, and talking about fun stuff for once! / I'll go first. / Wow! That is strong!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (purple sweater) hands the blue ceramic pipe to Character_P (thin frame, green jacket). Character_P takes a deep hit as she lights it for him, a look of warm nostalgia on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She held the pipe out and I took it. She lit it as I inhaled. The fragrant dark smell of it felt nostalgic. It transported me back in time. I felt like a kid again.

shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Hazy, warm afternoon indoor light, smoke particles hanging lazily in the air
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, purple oversized sweater) sit back on her couch, smiling and coughing, smoke drifting away, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The smoke hit my lungs like a flame-thrower. I tried to keep it in, but coughed it out, laughing. Yowsa! You're right, I don't need much of that! We sat back, and she put in a CD by Green Day. We listened.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater) twirls a strand of her brown hair around her finger, smiling thoughtfully. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) watches her with a happy, relaxed smirk, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Another turning point, a fork stuck in the road, the song sang. What does it mean? I asked. She said: none of us knows what will happen, but we make the best of things. I think I'm stoned, I laughed. We talked about so much.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater, light blue eyes) looks intently at Character_P, gesturing with her hands. Character_P (thin frame) listens, fascinated.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: She told me she could sometimes see people's auras. We are made of energy, and energy has been proven to never end. Well, energy fields surround our bodies. Different people emit different colors.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (purple sweater, light blue eyes) as she smiles warmly, looking with deep affection at Character_P.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: I can't see it right now, but once or twice I saw it – you are a beautiful color of light blue. To me, blue is peaceful, soothing, calm, and friendly. It's loyalty. You are a loyal person.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) raises his eyebrows in playful disbelief, then smiles. Character_S (purple sweater) grins, grabbing her purse, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You've been loyal to your Ma and brother your entire life. That proves it. I guess you're right. What color are you? I feel like I'm blue or purplish, denoting faith or mystical qualities. You mystified me! I said. Let's get ice cream! she wailed.

music_intent.style_description: Green Day-style acoustic fingerpicking transitioning into ethereal synthesizer energy-sweeps and a soothing solo violin
music_intent.mood_arc: Green Day Vibe → Fork in the Road → Auras and Colors → Light Blue

scenes.setting: Day 216 - Dairyland Ice Cream Stand (Exterior) - Conquering the Beast
scenes.summary: Day 216 - Dairyland Ice Cream Stand (Exterior) - Conquering the Beast — Totally! I had the munchies. We hit the sidewalk, heading to Dairyland. Out in the world, I felt conspicuous. Two stoned people trying to communicate with an unlike species. It freaked me out a little

 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma.

 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater) walk down the sidewalk, laughing playfully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Totally! I had the munchies. We hit the sidewalk, heading to Dairyland. Out in the world, I felt conspicuous. Two stoned people trying to communicate with an unlike species. It freaked me out a little.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) and Character_S (purple sweater) sit on a concrete bench at a white table, licking chocolate soft-serve ice cream from waffle cones, grinning, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I managed to order a soft-serve chocolate in a waffle cone, and we sat on a cement bench at a sticky table, eating and saying 'mmm'. I had no idea someone was watching us.

story_beats.visual_event: Bob (flannel shirt untucked, small beer belly, backward baseball cap) approaches, holding a smoking Marlboro. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) looks up, surprised and nervous, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked up, and there was Bob, Nick's buddy. Wow man! How are you? Bob said, hands in his pockets, flannel untucked. Haven't seen you since the big brawl at Joe's. Oh, hey man, I said, feeling out of place.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) introduces Character_S (short brown hair, purple sweater). Sionna stands and politely shakes Bob's hand, holding back a giggle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My life with Sionna and my life with Nick's friends had just collided. Bob, this is Sionna. Sionna, Bob. Sionna shook his hand. Nice to meet you. Bob chucked, did you know your boyfriend is famous?

story_beats.visual_event: Bob (backward cap) gestures animatedly, smoking. Character_S (purple sweater, eyes slightly red) smiles, highly amused. Character_P (thin frame) looks down, embarrassed.

audio.speaker: Bob
audio.dialogue: Your boyfriend single-handedly conquered the BEAST!

story_beats.visual_event: Bob (backward cap, flannel shirt) punches his own palm, mimicking a fight move. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) shakes his head, sighing deeply; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Bob
audio.dialogue: Man, you were in Nick's face! You moved like Bruce Lee, dog! / Everybody is talking. Sayin' he's a pussy, excuse my language. Sayin' he's not the same.

music_intent.style_description: Upbeat folk-acoustic guitar transitioning into slightly wavy, stoned synth notes, and a slow, lazy blues slide-guitar
music_intent.mood_arc: The Walk → Conspicuous → Bob's Entrance → Bruce Lee Rumors

scenes.summary: Day 216 / Night 216 - Dairyland / Sionna's House (Exterior/Interior) - Feud Resolution — Nuff said! / Man, you might get pissed and kick my ass! Tell Nick I want to hang sometime.

senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Transition from bright outdoor sunset at the ice cream stand to the cozy, loving nighttime of Sionna's bedroom
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Bob (backward cap, flannel shirt) laughs and slaps his thigh. Character_P (young man, thin frame, green canvas jacket) looks extremely concerned, staring off.

audio.speaker: Bob
audio.dialogue: Nuff said! / Man, you might get pissed and kick my ass! Tell Nick I want to hang sometime.

story_beats.visual_event: Bob waves, turning and walking down the street, flicking ashes from his Marlboro. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) and Character_S (purple sweater) watch him go, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I thought he would be at your Ma's begging the new king of the shit hill! Bob laughed. Just kidding. Tell him I want to hang. I said I would. Bob said goodbye to Sionna and walked away, flicking ashes.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) looking deeply troubled and worried as he licks his melting cone under the setting sun, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Wow, Sionna laughed. You have pretty interesting friends. He's NOT my friend, I said, he's Nick's. But something was nagging at my brain. Where was Nick? He hadn't talked to his friends or been at the gym. I had to find him.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, white tank top) sit on the edge of her bed, holding each other close, sharing a tender embrace in the cozy room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I decided it was time to end this feud and find Nick. But tonight, we went back to her house. We had a great time together. Great times go too fast.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame) and Character_S (short brown hair) sleep peacefully in each other's arms under the soft, dark bedsheets, smiling slightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We slept deeply, blissfully cocooned in her room, our hearts beating in perfect rhythm as the night slipped away.

music_intent.style_description: Melancholic solo cello and low synth pads transitioning to quiet reflective piano and a warm, tender acoustic guitar and flute theme
music_intent.mood_arc: Missing Brother → Bob's Farewell → A Nagging Brain → Loving Haven

scenes.setting: Day 220 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Boiled Dry
scenes.summary: Day 220 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Boiled Dry — Ma was becoming a handful, with all the other stuff on my plate. I had to constantly check her pills. Nick was never around, off the face of the earth. This morning I woke up. I smell something burnin

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Hazy morning kitchen light, smoke from a dry coffee pot catching sunbeams
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, white t-shirt, grey sweatpants) sits up on his bed, smelling the air, looking alarmed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma was becoming a handful, with all the other stuff on my plate. I had to constantly check her pills. Nick was never around, off the face of the earth. This morning I woke up. I smell something burning, can you smell it? she asked.

story_beats.visual_event: In the kitchen, Character_P (thin frame, white t-shirt) uses an oven mitt to throw a dry, smoking metal coffee pot into a sink of splashing, steaming water, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sure enough, the kitchen smelled like burning metal. The old coffeepot had boiled dry. I turned the burner off, grabbed it with an oven mitt, and threw it in the sink to cool. Ma! I yelled, you're going to burn the damn building down!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pink housecoat) sits in her living room chair, weeping helplessly. Character_P (thin frame) kneels beside her, holding her hands gently.

audio.speaker: Character_M
audio.dialogue: I did what? she started sniveling and crying. She had sudden realizations of her senility. I held her hands. Now Ma, come on, don't cry, I comforted. I can't remember anything anymore, she sobbed, I'm no good for anything.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame) hugs Character_M (pink housecoat, silver hair) tightly, kissing her cheek as her weeping slows, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma, really, stop. I had school in an hour. It's fine, Ma. Please stop. I hugged her. You're great Ma. I love you. She looked up. Nick doesn't love me, she said, he never comes to see me. Did I do something to make him mad?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, white t-shirt) smiles reassuringly at Character_M, smoothing her silver hair before standing up to head to the kitchen, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: No, of course not. I tell you what, I'll go check on him today. Make sure he's okay. She said her heart was a mess, pounding in her chest. I made a quick bowl of cereal. I survived on cereal and pizza. It was time to sort this out.

music_intent.style_description: Metallic chime loop transitioning into dissonant acoustic guitar chords, a weeping violin melody, and comforting soft acoustic guitar
music_intent.mood_arc: Morning Alarm → The Sink Splash → Dementia Tears → Pledge to Ma
