scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Transition from clinical college classroom daylight to shifting shadows inside a moving city bus
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: In an English classroom, a female teacher laughs loudly. Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) sits at his desk, fighting to stay awake, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had a tough time in English class. I was supposed to concentrate on modern fiction, but my mind drifted to last night. I could feel the soft sheets, the smell of lavender and citrus. I almost fell asleep.

story_beats.visual_event: The classroom empties. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) scoops his textbooks into his backpack, checking the wall clock which reads 3:45 PM, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The teacher cackled, 'Yes John, Lolita is a novel about a disturbed child corrupting an adult! Hahaha. Read Slaughterhouse-Five for Thursday!' It was 3:45. Time to check on Nick, like I promised Ma.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) sits on a city bus, staring thoughtfully out the window at the passing grey city buildings and streets, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I took the bus to Nick's apartment. I started to think what an ass he is. I had never thought of Nick this way before. Times were changing. He once was the center of my world, now he was just an annoying side-note.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P's face (thin frame) in the shifting bus window light, reflecting a mix of maturity, resolve, and sorrow, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna was the center of my world now. She deserved to be. She is hard-working, independent, and tough, but soft, light, and considerate. Nick's toughness was hard, dark, scary, and inconsiderate. My mind ran wild.

music_intent.style_description: Academic electric piano transitioning to dreamy acoustic guitar and citrus flute, then a steady driving electronic bus tempo
music_intent.mood_arc: Lolita Cackle → Dreaming of Lavender → The City Bus Ride → Two Paths / Outro

scenes.setting: Day 220 - Nick's Apartment (Interior) - Taming the Wild Dog
scenes.summary: Day 220 - Nick's Apartment (Interior) - Taming the Wild Dog — I entered Nick's 1920s brick building, with massive twelve-foot ceilings and ornate railings. I tapped lightly. Nick? Nick? Fearing I'd find him dead, I knocked louder. Then I heard a faint shuffling.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Dim, chaotic apartment lighting, shadows stretching across piles of clutter and garbage
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket) walks through a 1920s brick corridor with high ceilings, approaching Nick's apartment door. He pulls the bus cord and knocks, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I entered Nick's 1920s brick building, with massive twelve-foot ceilings and ornate railings. I tapped lightly. Nick? Nick? Fearing I'd find him dead, I knocked louder. Then I heard a faint shuffling.

story_beats.visual_event: The door creaks open a crack, held by a gold safety chain. Character_N (older brother, muscular build, disheveled crazy hair, pox-marked face) glares out suspiciously.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: What do YOU want? / What for?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, disheveled hair, dark grey t-shirt) closes and unlocks the chain, opening the door. Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) steps inside a highly cluttered living room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I told him Ma was worried, and we needed to talk. He squinted. Go home, haven't you done enough damage? Nick, please, let's not talk through a crack. He unlatched the chain. The living room was a total disaster area.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, dark grey t-shirt) paces furiously in the messy living room, waving a hand in absolute outrage. Character_P (thin frame) sits cautiously on a cluttered sofa.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: What do YOU think, moron? / You've ruined my life! I can't go anywhere. People are talking. Saying I'm a pussy, my little brother kicked my ass!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, dark grey t-shirt) points an angry finger right at Character_P's nose, looking wild and vengeful. Character_P (thin frame) keeps his posture calm, hands open.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: All because of your big mouth! Trying to be a hero. You piece of shit! You started some rumor to make yourself look good. My reputation is dog shit. Thanks a lot Bro, thanks a lot.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) speaks in a soft, slow, soothing manner, looking directly into Character_N's eyes.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick, man, whoa. Slow down. I didn't do anything, I said, using a soft voice, like trying to tame a wild dog. He snickered, grabbing a cold cup of coffee, microwaving it, pacing like an obsessed man looking for revenge.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build) takes a sip of his heated coffee and sneers bitterly, raising a finger mockingly at Character_P (thin frame), lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I explained the night was a blur, I just didn't want him beating that guy over nothing. I apologized sincerely. Well let me tell you something, Nick held up a finger. You... YOU... are not the brother I knew, man. You're not!

music_intent.style_description: Echoing corridor synth pad, high-hat ticking with stabs, heavy blues-rock distorted groove, dropping to slow soft piano and sharp mocking guitar riffs
music_intent.mood_arc: The 1920s Corridor → The Chain Lock → Nick's Chaos → Obsessed Fury

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, green canvas jacket, grey t-shirt) stands before Character_N (older brother, muscular build, disheveled crazy brown hair, dark grey t-shirt), pleading with his hands open, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick, I'm not like that. I'm working my ass off, and finding out how stupid I am. That's it, man. Nick just stared. I felt like a man on trial, waiting to hear if I had life without parole, or the death sentence.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, dark grey t-shirt) stands over Character_P (thin frame), looking massive and menacing in the dim room, pointing a finger.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I don't give a shit how hard you work or about your girlfriend. But you are my little brother. I care about you and Ma, and I'll let it slide. But don't you ever, EVER, interrupt me in public again. You got that?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) nods quickly, speaking softly. Character_N (muscular build) relaxes slightly, crossing his arms, looking satisfied, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Yes Nick, I got that. I'm smoothing it out with your gym buddies, telling them it was a stupid made-up story. Do they really think I could stop you? He smirked, yes, it's stupid. You could never stop me in a million years.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular, dark grey t-shirt) rubs his forehead, looking slightly suspicious but tired. Character_P (thin frame) watches him carefully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Will you see Ma? She's dying to see you. Yeah, I'll come tomorrow. Tell her I've been sick. She doesn't know about this, right? Of course not, I said, but Ma's losing her memory big-time. Today she cried over the burner.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, green jacket) walks out of Nick's apartment door, looking relieved as the door creaks shut.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: She'll probably have to go to an old folks' home one of these days. / Make sure you give her her meds, or she'll end up in the hospital.

music_intent.style_description: Low, tense electronic sub-bass drone and quiet electric piano chords transitioning to soothing acoustic guitar and sad cello
music_intent.mood_arc: Deafening Silence → The Verdict → Feeding the Beast → Ma's Future

scenes.setting: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Outhouses and Lightning
scenes.summary: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Outhouses and Lightning — On Halloween, my brothers would watch from the bushes and tip those outhouses over! We were plain hoodlums on the farm!

 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a

my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to

connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs.

school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It

 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t

were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.”

scenes.setting: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Sizing Up the Professor
scenes.summary: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Sizing Up the Professor — Ma and I were zoning to the tube when there was a knock, and the door opened by itself. In walked Nick. Oh, hi, I stammered. Suddenly I realized the fateful day had come. Nick and Sionna were meeting.

scenes.setting: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Verbal Showdown
scenes.summary: Day 221 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Verbal Showdown — Well Nick, I disagree. In the last forty years, degrees earned by women rose from 35 to 55 percent. We make up sixty percent of undergrads. This is encouraging, considering women juggle school, family

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 21 ---
weird doggie position, were my shirt kept falling down over my face. I had to finally tuck the 
shirt into my pants so I could see what was going on. I thought yoga sounded pretty easy. You 
stand, you sit, you lay down - whatever. What I found out was this crap was hard. During it I felt 
completely out of breath all the time, dizzy half the time, and just plain out of shape. My groins 
felt like someone pulled me apart, just like a stretch Armstrong. My lower back was aching, and 
my shoulders hurt. I couldn't do all the push-up things, so I felt like a wimp. 
 Here's the weirdest thing of all though - after it was over, I felt so relaxed, like I just 
woke up after sleeping all night. And talk about calm, I wouldn't care if someone called me or 
Nick a big pussy right to our faces. I just wouldn't care at all. What's that about? I didn't feel 
violent, or mad, or really anything. Just calm. Not that I'm the meanest person anyway, but I 
usually would stand up for myself for cryin’ out loud. 
 "You were really great at that" Sionna said. 
 "Are you kidding me?" 
 "No, you caught on very quickly, and I have the feeling you liked it. You did like it, 
didn't you?" 
 "Um, yeah. Yeah! I did like it! I just felt so dumb. I don't know why." 
 "That's pretty common for the first few times. You'll get the hang of it, and then you'll 
understand the process", she said. 
 "What do you mean, the process?" 
 "Well, it's true what they say. Your practice is your practice. The only thin
--- PAGE 22 ---
 "That's why they call it 'practice', haha." 
 We both smiled at that one. I guess if all I'm doing is practicing, then I never have to be 
perfect. Also, I guess no one is ever "done". I had to hurry up and go home to change for work. 
It was the best night of work ever. I can't explain it, but making pizzas didn't bother me. Cleaning 
up didn't bother me. The asshole customers didn't bother me. Nothing. I was just happy to be 
there, which I don't think has ever happened to me before, unless you count payday, when I was 
just happy to be getting a check.

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that?

--- PAGE 1 ---
 Nick and Me

1 
Big brother was mine. I’m not talking about that stupid book we had to read in junior 
high about how the world was gonna turn weird and everybody could see everything you were 
doing all the time. No, I’m talking about my real Big Brother. Nick was the guy everybody was 
scared of. He had a pair on him the size of steel wrecking balls. His face looked like a puzzle 
with the pieces held together with sticky stuff from a glue gun. He wasn’t ugly, just had a few 
brawls over the years to give him that rough look. It was like he rolled out of bed and forgot to 
comb his hair. A few scars here and there, chin and over his left eyebrow. When he walked in the 
bar, or the gym, people took notice, and man, did they move out of the way. I lived with him and 
Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. We lived downtown in a big brick apartment 
building on Kirk Street. A fifth-floor walk-up, so it was a real pain in the ass to haul up the 
groceries and stuff. Luckily, when I went to the store with Nick, he could load up with about six 
of those shopping bags and take them up in one trip, like he was in one of those “world’s 
strongest man” competitions, where they throw boulders over walls, or pull cars with just their 
teeth. I held the rest. 
 One time, when we went into Moxie’s, the gym down on the corner, one of those guys in 
there was doin’ preacher curls with a 40-pound bar on the curl bench. He was right in the middle 
of a set, but as soon 
--- PAGE 2 ---
weight lifting, but I aint gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. Hell, I aint gonna curl a 50-
pound weight probably ever. That’s okay though, Nick is my brother, like I said. I know he’ll 
stand up for me no matter what. 
 Milwaukee is where we live. It’s where we lived all of our lives. Right there, on Kirk 
Street in that fifth floor apartment. We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and 
set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids. One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right 
through old Mrs. Engel’s kitchen. Man, was she pissed. Mark was scared to death, but Nick 
stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it. She called my Ma, and Ma had to pay 
for the window. Somehow Nick gave her cash to pay for it about a week later. I have no idea 
where a 12-year old came up with sixty bucks, but I was sure proud of him for that. He never 
told Ma that it was really Mark who did it. None of us did. 
 The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking - you know? What I mean is we had 
these ancestors that came from Norway or Sweden or somewhere over there in Viking land and 
they came over here and stayed in Wisconsin. So, we are Vikings too, I guess. You never hear 
about Vikings, but they are the biggest bad-asses on the planet. I don’t care about gangsters from 
Mexico or China, or even Irish people. Hell, all they do is drink anyways. What I’m talking 
about are tall, strong people who survived in the middle of winter all the time. 
--- PAGE 3 ---
the way those Vikings used to live over there it makes me all proud. They had those huge swords 
and when somebody died, they floated them out to sea on a raft and set them on fire. That’s the 
way it should be. None of this taking up space with caskets and shit. Just set me on fire and float 
me out on a raft. It is so cool to see fire on top of water. 
 I guess that’s why people respect Nick and our family, even though dad took off.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck shirt, light blue eyes) stands politely, gesturing calmly as she speaks. Character_N (older brother, muscular build, slicked-back hair) sits stunned.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Well Nick, I disagree. In the last forty years, degrees earned by women rose from 35 to 55 percent. We make up sixty percent of undergrads. This is encouraging, considering women juggle school, family, and work.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_N (muscular build, slicked-back hair), his face turning red, looking completely speechless and humiliated.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: I don't see how education cannot be changed for the better by female professors. / You can't keep a good woman down.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build) tries to stammer a response, looking at Character_M (silver hair, pink housecoat). Character_P (thin frame, blue t-shirt) smiles widely, cheering silently.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: If you ask me, that's why the country is heading down the tubes! Women can't stay home with kids. No offense Ma.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (silver hair, pink housecoat) smiles warmly, pointing to the organized pill containers on her side table. Character_N (muscular build) watches, seething silently, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna walked to the kitchen. Inside I was jumping, yelling 'that's my girl!' She verbally beat the shit out of him. Nick was seething. Ma praised Sionna: thanks to Sionna, everything is straightening out nicely.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, dark blue button-down shirt) stands up to leave, delivering a passive-aggressive sneer. Character_S (short brown hair) walks back into the room.

audio.speaker: Character_N

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build) aggressively grabs Character_P (thin frame, blue t-shirt) by the shoulders, shaking him slightly before pulling him close.

audio.speaker: Character_N

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Take care of Ma.

scenes.setting: Day 250 - Narrator's Apartment / Library (Interior) - Athena and Menoetius
scenes.summary: Day 250 - Narrator's Apartment / Library (Interior) - Athena and Menoetius — It was the best of times, and the worst of times. I kept a low profile. Sionna never spoke about Nick, but he always had a snide remark. I kept my mouth shut. I spent my time in the library.

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, dark grey sweater, glasses) sits at a large wooden college library table, taking notes from massive old books, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It was the best of times, and the worst of times. I kept a low profile. Sionna never spoke about Nick, but he always had a snide remark. I kept my mouth shut. I spent my time in the library.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (older brother, muscular build, dark flannel shirt) sits beside Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, blue housecoat) in her apartment, over-attentively feeding her a treat, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick came around more to check on Ma, laying out her pills and bringing her treats. His behavior was over the top. She began to trust Nick again, doting on him. Nick was in, and I was out. Just as well.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P (thin frame, glasses) looking up from his book on Greek mythology with a peaceful, inspired expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was researching the Peloponnesian Wars and Greek mythology. As I read about Athena, my mind drifted to Sionna. They were both Goddesses of intelligence and skill – peaceful warriors ready for battle.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater) writes down a Greek name: 'Menoetius', then closes his textbook with a sigh of realization, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick, on the other hand, was like this God named Menoetius. Exceedingly prideful, hubristic, and impetuous. Was I in a tragic Greek play? Love wasn't competitive or demeaning. You live and learn.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame) exits the library, walking into the sunshine, looking older, more independent, and deeply peaceful, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma was our touchstone. If not for her, Nick and I would never see each other. I didn't have time for Nick anyway. Work, school, Sionna, Ma. That was it.

music_intent.mood_arc: The Two Cities → Competitive Doting → Athena the Warrior → Hubristic Menoetius

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, white t-shirt, jeans, light blue eyes) sits at her computer typing in her living room. Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt, jeans) walks in and hugs her from behind, smiling, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Lately I had more things nagging at me, because I had responsibilities. But today, a class was cancelled, the pizza joint was slow. I was free. I headed over to see Sionna. She was writing about creation stories on the computer.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, white t-shirt) points at comparative diagrams on her monitor, explaining eagerly. Character_P (thin frame, grey t-shirt) sits beside her, listening with fascination, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She was researching creation myths in different religions. We started discussing it, and stuff started to make sense. Like she said, the more you look into some things, the less you believe any of it can be true.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame) and Character_S (short brown hair) lie side-by-side on the living room rug, staring up at the ceiling, smiling in deep contemplation, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We talked about God, and time and space. The universe is so vast with so many galaxies. Not long ago, man couldn't grasp that the earth was round. What if what we believe now seems ludicrous in another hundred years?

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (pale skin, short brown hair, light blue eyes) as she looks sideways at Character_P, her face peaceful and filled with wisdom.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: People will laugh at us for thinking one religion was right, and others wrong. They'll laugh at our strict rules to save us from fire. Maybe, like she says, God is just energy. Energy is in all of us.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame) turns his head to look at Character_S (short brown hair, white t-shirt) on the rug, deeply in love, his eyes reflective, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It existed before time, and will go on forever. I couldn't even wrap my head around 'forever'. But looking at her, I felt like I was touching a piece of eternity right here.

story_beats.visual_event: The camera slowly pulls up and away from Character_P and Character_S lying on the rug, as warm spring sunlight bathes them in a golden, glowing rectangle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Does energy care? I don't know. But I knew that whatever energy bound us together, it was the strongest force in my world.

music_intent.style_description: Mystical synth pads and soft acoustic guitar, gentle piano, celestial bells, a soaring spiritual cello, and a timeless orchestral harmony
music_intent.mood_arc: Creation Myths → Ludicrous Dogma → Vast Universe → God is Energy

 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (older brother, muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket) stands in the apartment doorway, speaking. Character_P (thin frame, red pizza shop polo) looks on, jaw dropped, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The most crazy thing ever is that Nick took Ma to mass. He just popped up Saturday afternoon and said he would take her to St. Patrick's for four o'clock. I didn't even know what to say. Nick at church? My jaw dropped.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (silver hair, pink housecoat) is flustered, waving her hands. Character_S (short brown hair, black V-neck) smiles warmly, gently holding her shoulders.

audio.speaker: Character_M
audio.dialogue: What should I wear?

story_beats.visual_event: Outside, Character_N (muscular build, leather jacket) gently guides Character_M (silver hair, in a pretty blue floral dress) into the passenger seat of his polished Pontiac. Character_P (thin frame) watches from the porch, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: As they took off in his Pontiac, all I could see in my mind was lightning bolts hitting the aisle as Nick stepped up to genuflect. If he gave confession, we wouldn't see him until next Christmas. I had to think: why?

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_P's face (thin frame) as he looks on, deeply analytical and suspicious under the afternoon sun, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You don't just go from tough guy, leader of the pack, scary mother, to pet your Mom's head and escort her to church. What is that? Did I do something to Nick at the bar that changed him forever? I had to hope not.

story_beats.visual_event: The Pontiac drives away down the leafy street. Character_P (thin frame, red pizza polo) stands on the porch, a look of profound realization and emotional depth on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: After all, we love Nick the way he is. Fearless leader, warrior, Viking hero. And one mean son of a bitch. Down deep, he takes care of us in his own way. What would we be? What would I be, without Nick? No answer.

music_intent.mood_arc: Jaw on Chest → Flustered Ma → The Pontiac Drive → Lightning in the Aisle

I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was

Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old

with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills

--- PAGE 32 ---

morning and night”

suspiciously.

you get the right pill at the right time.”
