story_beats.visual_event: An old, red Toyota Civic hatchback winds up and down a beautiful, curving country road through rolling green pastures, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was riding in the passenger seat of Sionna's Toyota Civic hatchback. Old car, but functional always. Great gas mileage. The road wound up and down the soft hills of Eastern Wisconsin in a rhythmic, calming way.

story_beats.visual_event: The car drives past towering pine trees and maples under a bright blue sky with puffy white clouds. A deer dashes momentarily near the forest edge, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It looked like a postcard with the bright blue sky, a few cumulus clouds, pine trees, and maples showing their green leaves in the summer sun. Absolutely beautiful, reminding me of England's pasturelands.

story_beats.visual_event: Inside the car, Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, sunglasses) leans back comfortably in his seat, looking out the window, a peaceful smile on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It was the first week of summer break, and I was actually taking a break for once! School was out for the week before summer session started. Ma was in the hands of Nick the chameleon, and we were on our way.

story_beats.visual_event: The red Toyota Civic hatchback drives off into the golden horizon of the beautiful, sunny Wisconsin pastureland, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We were on the way to visit Sionna's parents, Mushroom and Ginger. I was excited, relaxed, and happy.

music_intent.style_description: Bright, driving folk-rock acoustic guitar and light drums, swelling beautiful violin, and warm electric piano
music_intent.mood_arc: Summer Break → Wisconsin Hills → The Hatchback → Summer Sun / Outro

scenes.setting: Day 270 - Road Trip / Eastern Wisconsin (Interior) - Mushroom and Ginger
scenes.summary: Day 270 - Road Trip / Eastern Wisconsin (Interior) - Mushroom and Ginger — They actually like being called Mushroom and Ginger? You're sure? I had a strange vision of two old hippies with smoke, moccasins, the works. Yep, just call them Mushroom and Ginger. They've gone by t

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Bright sunlight streaming through the passenger and driver windows of the hatchback
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Inside the car, Character_S (young woman, short brown hair, green tank top, yellow sun hat) drives, smiling. Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) talks to her, amused, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: They actually like being called Mushroom and Ginger? You're sure? I had a strange vision of two old hippies with smoke, moccasins, the works. Yep, just call them Mushroom and Ginger. They've gone by that since the 60s.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) steers the car, looking happy and nostalgic. Character_P (thin frame) smiles warmly at her.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: They wouldn't even respond to Robert and Margery. / Yes, raised right here in the good old Midwest, christened with proper names and everything!

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, light blue eyes) looking slightly sad and wistful as she stares through the front windshield.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Actually, my father's parents mentally divorced him. I remember seeing them once at the farm. A bad scene, yelling, grandmother crying. Her hair piled up in a beehive, cheap blue polyester dress.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) looks at Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) with deep empathy and concern as she continues driving.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: She looked at me through the screen door, tears streaming. She knew her husband would never mend ways with dad. They're both dead now. My mom's parents tried to be in my life, but Grandma and Grandpa just didn't get tofu.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) chuckles, her expression brightening. Character_P (thin frame) chuckles too, highly amused.

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: They also didn't get making yogurt, unpasteurized milk, drumming around a fire, you know. They discouraged belief in the Easter bunny, Santa, or tooth fairy. Elves, wood nymphs, the spiritual life of plants and trees, all ok!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) smiles, teasing Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) as the car drives down a sun-dappled road, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I guess you could say I never got my hopes up unnecessarily, she said. So, your parents are pagans, I smiled. She looked at me, Yea, I guess that's as close as we're going to get.

music_intent.style_description: Nostalgic acoustic guitar and soft synth pads with sad solo cello, thoughtful electric piano, and beautiful Celtic harp and flute
music_intent.mood_arc: Robert and Margery → Grandmother's Tears → Beehive Hair → Wood Nymphs

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Bright, beautiful, rustic afternoon sunlight illuminating a quiet country farmhouse and green gardens
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: The red Toyota Civic hatchback drives into a gravel driveway in Door County, pulling up next to a dilapidated old farmhouse with a small weathered barn, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We arrived at their farm in Door County. It was ten acres with a dilapidated old farmhouse, a little barn, a few chickens scratching in the dirt, and a cow. The usual farm stuff, but it felt incredibly peaceful.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) step out of the hatchback, stretching under the beautiful country sun, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I wondered about living out in the open, without a bunch of people around. It felt like another world, clean and quiet. Sionna told me they grow their own food, can it for winter, and drink unpasteurized milk.

story_beats.visual_event: Mushroom (elderly hippie man, long grey beard, tie-dye shirt, sandals) and Ginger (elderly hippie woman, long grey hair in braids, long floral dress) wave happily from the porch, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: They adored Sionna, their only child. As we walked up the wooden steps, two old hippies stepped out. Mushroom had a long grey beard and a tie-dye shirt, and Ginger wore a beautiful long floral dress. They waved with pure joy.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (green tank top) runs up the steps, sharing a massive, loving hug with Ginger and Mushroom on the wooden porch. Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) watches, smiling warmly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I watched Sionna hug them tightly. It was a beautiful, loving reunion. Real eggs off the farm, unpasteurized milk, and total acceptance. You are more than fine, I told her in my mind. You are perfect.

story_beats.visual_event: Mushroom and Ginger smile, shaking Character_P's (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) hand warmly and welcoming him onto the porch as the camera pans slowly across the green pasture, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: They welcomed me with open arms. No judgment, no demeaning jokes, no competition. Just warm, earthy kindness. For a brief moment, I felt like we had found a piece of heaven.

music_intent.style_description: Earthy, rustic acoustic guitar and fiddle, happy hand percussion, warm sitar-folk slide guitar, and glockenspiel
music_intent.mood_arc: The Dilapidated House → The Barnyard → Meeting Mushroom → Pure Unpasteurized

scenes.setting: Day 270 / Night 270 - Mushroom and Ginger's Farm (Interior) - Murphy's Law / Introduction to Act Three
scenes.summary: Day 270 / Night 270 - Mushroom and Ginger's Farm (Interior) - Murphy's Law / Introduction to Act Three — We spent a wonderful evening talking, eating organic farm food, and watching the embers of the brick fireplace glow. I felt incredibly safe, as if my troubles had been left hundreds of miles behind.

people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

Total Pages: 94
--- PAGE 1 ---
 Nick and Me

1 
Big brother was mine. I’m not talking about that stupid book we had to read in junior 
high about how the world was gonna turn weird and everybody could see everything you were 
doing all the time. No, I’m talking about my real Big Brother. Nick was the guy everybody was 
scared of. He had a pair on him the size of steel wrecking balls. His face looked like a puzzle 
with the pieces held together with sticky stuff from a glue gun. He wasn’t ugly, just had a few 
brawls over the years to give him that rough look. It was like he rolled out of bed and forgot to 
comb his hair. A few scars here and there, chin and over his left eyebrow. When he walked in the 
bar, or the gym, people took notice, and man, did they move out of the way. I lived with him and 
Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. We lived downtown in a big brick apartment 
building on Kirk Street. A fifth-floor walk-up, so it was a real pain in the ass to haul up the 
groceries and stuff. Luckily, when I went to the store with Nick, he could load up with about six 
of those shopping bags and take them up in one trip, like he was in one of those “world’s 
strongest man” competitions, where they throw boulders over walls, or pull cars with just their 
teeth. I held the rest. 
 One time, when we went into Moxie’s, the gym down on the corner, one of those guys in 
there was doin’ preacher curls with a 40-pound bar on the curl bench. He was right in the middle 
of a set, but as soon 
--- PAGE 2 ---
weight lifting, but I aint gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. Hell, I aint gonna curl a 50-
pound weight probably ever. That’s okay though, Nick is my brother, like I said. I know he’ll 
stand up for me no matter what. 
 Milwaukee is where we live. It’s where we lived all of our lives. Right there, on Kirk 
Street in that fifth floor apartment. We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and 
set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids. One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right 
through old Mrs. Engel’s kitchen. Man, was she pissed. Mark was scared to death, but Nick 
stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it. She called my Ma, and Ma had to pay 
for the window. Somehow Nick gave her cash to pay for it about a week later. I have no idea 
where a 12-year old came up with sixty bucks, but I was sure proud of him for that. He never 
told Ma that it was really Mark who did it. None of us did. 
 The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking - you know? What I mean is we had 
these ancestors that came from Norway or Sweden or somewhere over there in Viking land and 
they came over here and stayed in Wisconsin. So, we are Vikings too, I guess. You never hear 
about Vikings, but they are the biggest bad-asses on the planet. I don’t care about gangsters from 
Mexico or China, or even Irish people. Hell, all they do is drink anyways. What I’m talking 
about are tall, strong people who survived in the middle of winter all the time. 
--- PAGE 3 ---
the way those Vikings used to live over there it makes me all proud. They had those huge swords 
and when somebody died, they floated them out to sea on a raft and set them on fire. That’s the 
way it should be. None of this taking up space with caskets and shit. Just set me on fire and float 
me out on a raft. It is so cool to see fire on top of water. 
 I guess that’s why people respect Nick and our family, even though dad took off.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Transition from cozy, dim oil-lamp light inside the rustic farmhouse to a dark, rainy night outside, casting moody reflections
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Inside the cozy farmhouse living room, Character_P (28 years old, thin frame, light blue t-shirt) sits on a woolen rug by a brick fireplace, watching a fire crackle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We spent a wonderful evening talking, eating organic farm food, and watching the embers of the brick fireplace glow. I felt incredibly safe, as if my troubles had been left hundreds of miles behind.

story_beats.visual_event: Outside, rain begins to fall, tapping heavily on the farmhouse window pane. Inside, Character_P (thin frame) looks up, his expression turning solemn, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But this is Murphy's Law: whatever you think is going to happen is not what happens. Basically, it's pure irony. Like that song by Morissette – fly in your Chardonnay, rain on your wedding day...

story_beats.visual_event: PRESENT-DAY FRAMING DEVICE: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater) sits in a dark, moody room, staring out a rain-streaked window, looking deeply melancholic, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Just when you think everything is going your way, suddenly, it isn't. It makes me want to wake up pessimistic. Better to feel shitty and have things turn out well, than wake up happy and have things go to shit.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on the present-day Character_P (older, black sweater) as he sighs heavily, rubbing his eyes, a look of profound grief and memory in his expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: And that is exactly what happened. Everything went to shit. It's such a long story. I guess I'll just relive this nightmare. I am a guy. I always grew up not having any motives. I trusted people. I took them for their word.

story_beats.visual_event: Present-day Character_P (older) looks up, and the reflection of Character_S (short brown hair, light blue eyes) appears softly behind him in the window pane as she gently places a hand on his shoulder, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: When you're a guy living by yourself, you have no reason to distrust or analyze. I just didn't think like that. Then, when a woman enters your life, you start to see things from a different angle. The nightmare had begun.

music_intent.style_description: Tranquil ambient keyboard pads and soft fingerpicked guitar, transitioning into a low ominous electronic drone, a steady Alanis-style dramatic rock beat, and tense cello
music_intent.mood_arc: Tranquil Evening → The Storm Pre-echo → Alanis Vibe → Reliving the Nightmare

scenes.setting: Night 270 - Mushroom and Ginger's Farm (Interior) - The Call
scenes.summary: Night 270 - Mushroom and Ginger's Farm (Interior) - The Call — It was three in the morning. A sound cut through the silence. At first I thought it was a bird, but then I realized it was the old rotary phone in the hallway ringing.

they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.”

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Moody, dim interior with flickering shadows cast by dying embers in the fireplace, interrupted by a single harsh, cold hallway light
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. sits up suddenly in bed in the dim rustic bedroom, looking startled as a phone rings, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It was three in the morning. A sound cut through the silence. At first I thought it was a bird, but then I realized it was the old rotary phone in the hallway ringing.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. walks down the dark wooden hallway toward the glowing rotary phone on a small table, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My heart was pounding in my chest. You never get a phone call at three in the morning for good news. My feet felt cold on the floorboards as I reached for the phone.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. reaches out a shaking hand to lift the black rotary receiver from its cradle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I picked up the heavy plastic receiver. Hello? I muttered, my voice raspy with sleep. The static on the line hissed back at me, filled with empty space.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. holds the phone to his ear, his face tight with rising dread under the cold hallway light, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then I heard Nick's voice. It didn't sound like Nick. It sounded tiny, shaking, and fragile. Little bro? he said. It's Ma. She's in trouble.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. listens intently, his eyes widening in shock and disbelief as he hears Nick's voice on the line, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He said she had collapsed in the kitchen. She wasn't breathing right. He had called the ambulance. They were taking her to Milwaukee General as fast as they could.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. leans heavily against the hallway's wooden paneling, closing his eyes as his face contorts with pain, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I felt the blood drain from my face. My knees buckled slightly, and I had to lean against the wood wall for support. The hallway seemed to spin around me.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. looks up blankly in the hallway, clutching the phone with white knuckles, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Where is she now? I asked, my voice barely a whisper. Nick said she was in the Intensive Care Unit, hooked up to machines. They didn't know if she would make it.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. stands there in the dark hallway, the cold receiver pressed to his ear, looking hollow, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stood there in the dark hallway, the cold receiver pressed to my ear, listening to the hum of the long-distance line, unable to form words.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. appears in the bedroom doorway behind Character_P, looking concerned and alert, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna came out of the bedroom, rubbing her eyes in the dim light, looking at me with growing concern. She knew instantly that something was terribly wrong.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. looks over his shoulder, his lips moving silently as he delivers the bad news, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked at her, my eyes wide with terror. It's Ma, I whispered. She's in the hospital. It's bad, Sionna. Really bad. My voice broke on her name.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. steps quickly down the hallway, wrapping her arms around Character_P in a comforting embrace, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Without a word, Sionna stepped forward and wrapped her arms around me, holding me tight as the realization hit me. Her warmth was the only thing keeping me anchored.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. holds Character_P's hands, speaking to him with calm, fierce determination

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She immediately took charge. We have to go, she said gently but firmly. Pack your bag. I'll get the car keys. We're driving back to Milwaukee right now.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. throws clothes clumsily into a dark duffel bag on the unmade bed in the rustic bedroom, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I nodded slowly, my mind completely numb. I felt like I was moving through thick gelatin, unable to think or react, packing items without even seeing them.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. grabs a light jacket from a chair and holds the car keys, looking determined, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna was already at the door, her yellow sun hat left behind on the table, holding her keys and looking at me with fierce, unwavering support.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. zips the duffel bag, his hands visibly trembling in the dim bedroom light, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I zipped the duffel bag, my hands shaking so hard I could barely pull the metal zipper. Every muscle in my body was tense, waiting for a blow.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. hurry out the wooden screen door of the farmhouse, carrying their bags onto the dark porch, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We ran down the porch steps into the cool night air. The peaceful Wisconsin farm now felt like a distant, cruel memory. The night was dark and silent.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the dim, rustic wooden farmhouse hallway. sit inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback, staring through the front windshield as headlights flicker on, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We got into the Toyota Civic. Sionna started the engine, and the headlights flickered on, cutting through the country darkness. We backed out onto the main road.

music_intent.style_description: Low, ominous ambient drone with tense violin tremolo and slow, rhythmic acoustic guitar stabs, building into high-register panic piano keys
music_intent.mood_arc: Silence Shattered → The Phone Rings → Tense Tremolos → Nick's Voice

scenes.setting: Night 270 - Road Trip / Rainy Highway (Exterior) - The Panic Drive
scenes.summary: Night 270 - Road Trip / Rainy Highway (Exterior) - The Panic Drive — The rain started about twenty miles outside of Sturgeon Bay. It didn't just rain – it poured. The water came down in heavy, blinding sheets on the glass.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Dark, moody night highway illuminated only by sweeping headlight beams and the constant, rhythmic sweep of windshield wipers reflection
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. sit inside the red Toyota Civic as rain sheets down heavily on the windshield, wipers sweeping fast, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The rain started about twenty miles outside of Sturgeon Bay. It didn't just rain – it poured. The water came down in heavy, blinding sheets on the glass.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. looks out the passenger window into the blackness, his face reflected in the rain-streaked window, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stared out into the pitch-black fields. Every time the lightning flashed, the trees looked like reaching skeleton arms. The road was a slick black snake.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. grips the steering wheel tightly, staring intently through the rain-streaked windshield, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna kept her eyes glued to the white lines on the asphalt. She didn't say anything, but I could see the muscles in her jaw working, tight with focus.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. sit in the car cabin as dashboard lights cast a soft green glow on their tense faces, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The only light was the dim green glow of the dashboard and the headlights cutting through the gray wall of water. The engine hummed a steady, anxious tune.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. squeezes his eyes shut, rubbing his forehead as if trying to block out a terrible thought, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My mind was spinning out of control. I kept thinking about Ma lying in that hospital bed, hooked up to cold plastic tubes. It felt like a physical weight in my chest.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. reaches across the center console to squeeze Character_P's hand comforting him

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Hey. / She's strong, P. She's survived so much already. Don't lose hope yet. Just breathe.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. looks down at their joined hands, swallowing hard as he tries to calm his rapid breathing, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I nodded, but my throat was too tight to reply. I gripped her hand back like it was a life preserver in a stormy ocean. I couldn't let go.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. stares at the spinning numbers of the car odometer as they slowly increase, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Every mile marker we passed felt like a tick of a giant clock. We were moving as fast as the old Toyota could go, but it felt like we were standing still.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. adjusts the windshield wiper speed, squinting through a sudden splash of water from a passing truck, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: A huge semi-truck roared past us in the opposite direction, throwing a massive wave of dirty water over our hood. The Civic shook, but Sionna held it steady.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. watches the raindrops slide horizontally across the passenger window pane, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I watched the drops of water race across the side glass, merging and disappearing into the dark. I wondered if Nick was sitting by himself at the hospital.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. leans his head back against the seat headrest, staring up at the dark roof of the car cabin, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick was always the one who dealt with the physical stuff, the violence, the action. But a hospital? That wasn't his arena. He must be terrified in there.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. glances at him with deep love and worry, her face soft in the dashboard light, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna checked her mirrors and accelerated slightly as the rain began to taper off into a light, gray drizzle. The sky in the east was starting to turn a pale gray.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. sits up, watching the distant city lights of Milwaukee appear on the horizon through the windshield, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: As the dawn broke, we finally saw the distant lights of Milwaukee reflecting off the wet pavement. My stomach did a slow, painful flip at the sight.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. look ahead as the car exits the highway onto a city ramp, driving past quiet streets, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The city was completely silent, asleep. It felt strange that the world was just carrying on, normal and quiet, while our entire lives were about to crash.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. steers the car onto the street leading to Milwaukee General Hospital, her face resolute, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna turned onto the main avenue leading to the hospital. The big brick buildings rose up ahead of us like cold monuments under the gray morning sky.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in a rain-swept highway at night inside the red Toyota Civic hatchback. looks up at the towering, sterile hospital building as the Civic pulls into the parking lot, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We pulled into the parking lot. The red Civic came to a stop, the engine turning off with a soft hiss. I stared at the emergency entrance, unable to move.

music_intent.style_description: Steady, driving drum beat representing the car's engine, overlaid with a tense, weeping solo viola and cold, wet-sounding synth textures
music_intent.mood_arc: The Storm Outside → Wiper Rhythm → Viola Weeps → Engine Rumble

scenes.setting: Day 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - The Waiting Room
scenes.summary: Day 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - The Waiting Room — We walked through the sliding glass doors. The air inside smelled of alcohol, bleach, and old coffee. The fluorescent lights buzzed overhead, harsh and cold.
