get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Harsh, buzzing fluorescent overhead lights casting a sterile, pale green pallor over vinyl chairs and tiled floors
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt) and Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top) walk through sliding glass doors into a sterile hospital lobby, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We walked through the sliding glass doors. The air inside smelled of alcohol, bleach, and old coffee. The fluorescent lights buzzed overhead, harsh and cold.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) sits slumped in a blue vinyl chair in the waiting room, his face buried in his hands, looking completely defeated, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I saw him immediately. Nick. He was slumped in a corner vinyl chair under a dead fern, his head in his hands. He looked so small in his leather jacket.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) walks slowly toward Nick in the sterile waiting room, his footsteps echoing on the tiles, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I walked over to him, my sneakers squeaking on the polished linoleum floor. Every step felt like lifting lead weights. Nick? I said softly.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) as he looks up, his eyes bloodshot and rimmed with red, his reddish-brown hair messy and unstyled, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He looked up. His eyes were bloodshot, and there were dark circles under them. For the first time, I saw fear in my brother's eyes. Real fear.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) and Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) stand face to face as Character_P sits in the adjacent chair, leaning forward, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He didn't make a sarcastic joke. He didn't puff out his chest. He just looked at me. P, he said, his voice raspy. She's in there. They won't let me see her.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) stands back slightly, holding her jacket in the waiting room, watching the brothers with deep compassion, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna stood a few feet back, giving us space, but her presence was a quiet anchor in the background. She looked at Nick with gentle, sorrowful eyes.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) shakes his head, running a hand through his reddish-brown hair in pure frustration, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I found her on the kitchen floor, P, Nick whispered, his voice cracking. She was just lying there. I didn't know what to do. I tried to lift her, but she was limp.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) puts a gentle hand on Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans)'s leather-clad shoulder, squeezing it tightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I put my hand on his shoulder. It was the first time I had initiated physical contact with him in years. You did the right thing, Nick. You called the ambulance.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) looks down at his hands, his fingers interlaced and shaking slightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: They took her in the ambulance, he said, staring at his palms. I followed in the Pontiac. I ran three red lights. I thought she was going to die in my car.

story_beats.visual_event: CLOSE-UP on Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans)'s shaking hands, raw and calloused, held open in front of him, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked at his hands – the hands that had won dozens of bar fights, the hands that had protected me. Right now, they were just shaking, empty and weak.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) walks over, gently placing a paper cup of water in Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans)'s hand with a warm smile, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna stepped forward and gently handed Nick a small paper cup of water. Drink this, Nick, she said softly. You need to stay hydrated. He took it numbly.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) takes a tiny sip of water, looking up at Sionna with quiet, uncharacteristic gratitude.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Thanks, Sionna.

story_beats.visual_event: The closed metal doors of the Intensive Care Unit sit in the hospital corridor under a bright exit sign, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We sat in silence. The double doors of the Intensive Care Unit stared back at us, cold and impassive. Every few minutes, a nurse in blue scrubs would walk through.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) leans back in the vinyl chair, staring up blankly at the buzzing fluorescent light fixture.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I always thought I could protect us from anything, P. / But I can't fight a stroke. I can't punch a disease.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) looks at Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans), feeling a sudden surge of deep, adult understanding of his brother's burden, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It was the most honest thing he had ever said. He had spent his whole life being our shield, and now the shield was useless. I felt a wave of deep love for him.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) sits beside Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), holding his hand, her shoulder resting against his comforting him, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna sat down next to me, taking my hand. We were three young people sitting in a cold room, waiting for a doctor to tell us if our world was going to end.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans), and Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) all look up as a doctor in a green scrub suit walks toward them, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Suddenly, the double doors swung open. A doctor in green scrubs and a white coat stepped out, holding a clipboard. He looked around and walked toward us.

music_intent.style_description: Cold, sterile synthesizer pads with a slow, mechanical acoustic guitar pluck, a hollow solo oboe, and soft ambient chimes
music_intent.mood_arc: Fluorescent Buzz → Slumped Figure → Hollow Oboe → Heavy Steps

scenes.setting: Day 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - ICU / Life Support
scenes.summary: Day 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - ICU / Life Support — The doctor led us into the Intensive Care Unit. The room was dim, filled with the steady, rhythmic hum and hiss of machines. It sounded like an alien spaceship.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Dim, cold room dominated by the glowing green and blue graphs of medical monitors and the soft orange light of a small bedside lamp
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. stares tearfully at her from the bedside, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The doctor led us into the Intensive Care Unit. The room was dim, filled with the steady, rhythmic hum and hiss of machines. It sounded like an alien spaceship.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. lies motionless with her silver hair spread across the pillow, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: And there she was. Ma. She looked so incredibly small in that big metal bed. There was a thick plastic tube in her mouth, connected to a bellows machine.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. strokes her pale arm with absolute gentleness, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I walked over to the side of the bed. Her skin was pale, almost translucent, and her silver hair was spread across the white pillow. I took her hand. It felt cool.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. looks up at the heart rate monitor, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick stood on the other side. He didn't touch her at first, just stared at her face, his arms hanging limp at his sides. He looked like he was in shock.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. looks down, eyes filling with tears, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Hey Ma, I whispered, leaning close to her ear. It's P. I'm here. We're both here, Ma. Nick is here too. Her eyelids didn't even flutter. Nothing.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. gently holds her small, cool hand

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Hey Ma.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. watches the chest rise and fall rhythmically with the machine's hiss, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The ventilator hissed, rising and falling in her chest in a slow, mechanical rhythm. It was doing all the work. Her chest rose, then fell with a click.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. stares at her serene, quiet face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I watched the green lines on the monitor. They bounced in a steady, synthetic rhythm. It felt so artificial, as if her life was just a drawing on a screen.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. bends his head down to weep, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick bent over the bed, his forehead resting against the metal side rail. I could see his broad shoulders shaking under his leather jacket. He was crying silently.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. wipes away a tear, looking at his brother, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had never seen Nick cry. Not when he was beaten, not when he was angry. Seeing him sob by Ma's bed broke something inside of me. I let my own tears fall.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. looks at the quiet face, holding his breath, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma looked completely peaceful, as if she was just taking a deep afternoon nap. No worries, no stress, no flustered questions about what dress to wear.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. kisses her cool, soft forehead in farewell, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I leaned down and kissed her forehead. She smelled faint, like her favorite lavender soap, but underneath it was the sharp, metallic smell of the hospital.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. wipes his eyes, his shoulders shaking

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick looked up, his face wet with tears, staring at P with raw vulnerability.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. stares back at his brother with raw emotion

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: P, she's not there. / That's not Ma. Ma is gone. I don't know where she is.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. leans close to her ear to whisper comfort, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She's still here, Nick, I said, though my voice was trembling. She can hear us. The doctors say they can hear you. Just talk to her. Tell her you're here.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. holds her cool hand against his cheek

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Ma, I'm here. / I took care of the house, Ma. I took you to mass, remember? St. Patrick's. You looked so pretty. I'm right here.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. stands in silent vigil beside the bed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We stood there, holding her hands, one brother on each side, the machines humming their endless, cold song. We were completely united in our vigil.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, equipment-filled ICU room. looks up as a nurse steps into the doorway, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We knew the next few days would decide everything. But standing there, holding Ma's hands, I knew that whatever happened, we were in this together.

music_intent.style_description: Slow, rhythmic synthesizer pulse mimicking a heartbeat, overlaid with a soaring, tragic solo violin and delicate, weeping piano notes
music_intent.mood_arc: The ICU Pulse → Tragic Entrance → Delicate Piano → The Machine's Breath

scenes.setting: Night 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - Sibling Vigil
scenes.summary: Night 271 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - Sibling Vigil — Night fell over the hospital, wrapping the room in deep shadows. Sionna had gone down to the cafeteria to get us some lukewarm coffee, leaving the two of us alone.

but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Deep night shadows with only the rhythmic, blue-green glow of the heart monitor casting long, quiet shapes on the walls
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. sit in plastic chairs on opposite sides of the room as night falls outside the window, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Night fell over the hospital, wrapping the room in deep shadows. Sionna had gone down to the cafeteria to get us some lukewarm coffee, leaving the two of us alone.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. leans back in his chair, rubbing his eyes from exhaustion, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I sat in the vinyl armchair, my bones aching with exhaustion. Nick sat opposite, staring at his boots. He hadn't spoken for over an hour.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. stares down at his boots, looking reflective

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: P, remember that winter we lived in that drafty apartment on Main Street? The one where the furnace broke?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks over at his brother, listening with a gentle expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked at him, surprised. Yeah, I remember. We slept in our coats for three days. You told me it was an indoor camping adventure to keep me from crying.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. smiles softly, a fleeting moment of warmth in his eyes

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Yeah. / I stole a bunch of wood from that construction site down the road and made a campfire in the yard. We roasted hotdogs.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. nods slowly, remembering a happy childhood moment, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I laughed softly. We did. Ma was so mad about the stolen wood, but she ate the hotdogs anyway. You always made sure we had something, Nick.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. frowns slightly, looking at his large calloused hands

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I didn't know how to do anything else, P. / I was just a kid, but I knew I had to feed you. I used to sneak cheese from the store.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. speaks with absolute sincerity, his voice low

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Of course I did. You were my little brother. That was my job.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks at the older brother, feeling a deep wave of respect, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stared at him, seeing the fierce, desperate boy he had been. He had carried the weight of our survival on his shoulders, and I had judged him for his rough edges.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. shifts in his seat, looking at Ma's quiet form, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I'm sorry, Nick, I said quietly. I'm sorry I was so critical of you. I thought you just liked trouble. I didn't see what you were actually doing.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks down at his hands, speaking softly

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Don't be stupid, P. / You were the smart one. You were supposed to study, not worry about food.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. shakes his head, looking surprised but pleased

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I always knew you'd get out of this town, P, he murmured. You have a beautiful brain. You're going to write books. I'm just a brawler. But you, you're special.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. nods in agreement, his face filled with genuine emotion, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I felt a massive lump form in my throat. I'm not special, Nick. I'm just your brother. We're both Ma's boys. That's all that matters.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks out the dark window reflecting their shapes

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Yeah. We are. / She loved you so much, P. She used to show your report cards to anyone who would look.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks back at the bed, feeling the weight of their bond

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: She was so proud of you, P. Even when she was flustered, she'd whisper to me: P is going to make us proud. He's going to be a real writer.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. reaches out to touch Ma's foot under the blanket, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I squeezed my eyes shut as the tears came again, warm and fast. I wished I could tell her that I was proud of her too. I wished I had said it more.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. sits forward, his hands clasped between his knees

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: You'll have your chance, P. / She knows. Mothers always know. She's resting now. Let's just sit with her.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the dim ICU room in the quiet night. looks up as the door handle begins to move, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We sat together in the quiet room, the silent bond of brotherhood stronger than any machine, waiting together for the morning sun.

music_intent.style_description: Whispering, atmospheric synthesizer pads with a very soft, sparse fingerpicked acoustic guitar and a gentle, comforting flute theme
music_intent.mood_arc: Night Falls → Vinyl Chairs → Main Street Memories → Stealing Wood

 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He took off his glasses and looked at us. His eyes were tired. I knew before he even spoke that there was no miracle waiting for us.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. listens intently, his hands tightly clasped on his lap, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.dialogue: The stroke was massive, he said, his voice flat and clinical. It affected her brainstem. The damage is irreversible, boys. She cannot recover.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. adjusts his gold wire-rimmed glasses, looking grave

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: But she's breathing!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. looks at P, his forehead furrowing as he hears the diagnosis, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The doctor explained that without the machines, her body would shut down. She has no brain activity. It's only the ventilator keeping her heart beating.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. nods slightly as the doctor explains, his heart sinking, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I nodded slowly. I had known it, but hearing a professional say it made it a hard, concrete wall. There was no climbing over it.

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: What are you saying? / You're just going to give up on her?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. looks up, his jaw set in stubborn denial, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We don't give up, son, the doctor said gently, not backing down from Nick's anger. But there is a point where medical treatment becomes unnecessary suffering.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. leans forward on the desk, explaining gently but firmly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He looked at both of us, his face soft. You need to think about what she would want. Would she want to live like this? Hooked to a machine?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. squeezes his eyes shut, trying to process the medical terms, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I thought about Ma. Ma, who was always flustered about the small stuff, but had a fierce, independent dignity. She would hate this.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. shakes his head, looking completely resistant to the truth, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She wouldn't want to be a vegetable, I said quietly, my voice shaking. Nick looked at me, his eyes blazing. Shut up, P! You don't know what she wants!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. looks at Nick, trying to offer silent comfort

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: She's our mother! / We don't pull the plug on our mother! We fight for her!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. speaks with professional compassion, his hands flat on the desk

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick, there is no fight left, the doctor said, his voice a quiet, heavy hammer. The fight is over. Now it's about letting her go with peace.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. watches the doctor's mouth move, the words feeling distant, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He told us to take some time, talk to each other, and make a decision. He patted Nick's shoulder as he walked out, leaving us in the quiet office.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. grips his armrest, looking like he wants to punch the wall, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick stood there, his fists clenched, his breathing ragged. He looked like a cornered animal, ready to lash out at anyone who got close.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. looks at his brother, his eyes wide with a painful realization, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stood up and faced him. We have to talk about this, Nick, I said softly. We can't keep her alive for our sake if she's gone.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the small walnut-walled doctor's office. nods slowly to the doctor, acknowledging the finality, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He didn't reply. He just pushed past me, slamming the door behind him. I sat back down in the office, alone with the humming of the air vents.

music_intent.style_description: Cold, clinical piano chords accompanied by a low, dragging bass drone and a weeping solo oboe theme
music_intent.mood_arc: Medical Office → The Doctor's Voice → Irreversible Damage → Brainstem Stroke

scenes.setting: Night 272 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - The Decision
scenes.summary: Night 272 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - The Decision — I found Nick in the west wing corridor. He was pacing back and forth like a caged beast, his heavy boots squeaking on the tiles, his leather jacket rustling.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Moody, cold blue-grey night light flooding through a large corridor glass window pane, casting long shadows
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. stand in the quiet hallway with pale yellow walls, speaking with hushed intensity, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I found Nick in the west wing corridor. He was pacing back and forth like a caged beast, his heavy boots squeaking on the tiles, his leather jacket rustling.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. paces back and forth, rubbing his temples, looking completely overwhelmed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I didn't say anything at first. I just stood there, letting him run out of energy. He needed to burn off the anger before he could face the truth.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. stands still, watching him, his face reflecting sorrow and heavy resolve

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: P, they want us to kill her. That's what it is. Removing the machine is killing her. I can't do that.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. stops pacing, staring out the glass pane of the corridor window, shoulders tight, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I walked over and stood beside him by the window. The city below was busy, people driving to work, living normal lives. It felt so incredibly distant.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. walks over to stand beside him, looking out the same window, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It's not killing her, Nick, I said softly, my voice surprisingly steady. Her spirit is already gone. She's just being held here by plastic and electricity.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. turns to face him, his eyes filled with a desperate, childish panic

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: What if they're wrong? / What if she wakes up? What if she's trapped inside her mind, screaming for us to help?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. speaks with gentle but firm logic, placing a hand on his arm

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I shook my head. The doctor showed us the brain scans, Nick. There's nothing left. She's not trapped. She's already at peace. We're the ones holding her back.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. pulls his arm away, shaking his head in stubborn resistance

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: But she's our mother! he choked out, his voice cracking. We're supposed to protect her! I'm supposed to protect both of you! That's what I do!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. takes a deep breath, trying to control his own breaking voice, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I placed my hand on his arm. You have protected us, Nick. Your whole life. But protecting her now means letting her go with dignity, not keeping her as a machine.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. looks down at his clenched fists, his jaw trembling, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She would hate being hooked up to these things, Nick. You know how independent she is. She'd be so embarrassed to have people feeding and cleaning her.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. speaks softly, pleading with him to see her dignity

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick looked down at his boots. His chest was heaving. He was trying so hard to hold onto the brawler, the tough guy, but the brawler had no power here.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. leans his head against the glass pane, closing his eyes as a tear slides down, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I saw a tear run down his cheek, dripping onto his leather sleeve. He looked so incredibly young, so vulnerable. I felt my own heart break for him.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. steps closer, wrapping a strong arm around his shaking shoulders, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stepped forward and wrapped my arms around him. It was a real hug, the kind we hadn't shared since we were little kids sleeping in the freezing cold bedroom.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. hugs his brother back, burying his face in his shoulder as he finally weeps, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick didn't push me away. He grabbed onto my shirt, burying his face in my shoulder, and sobbed. He wept with a raw, gasping grief that shook his entire body.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. holds him tight in the empty, silent hallway, tears in his own eyes, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I held him tight, letting him cry, feeling the deep, ancient bond of our blood. We were the only ones who knew what it was to be Ma's boys.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. pulls back slightly, wiping his face with the back of his hand, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It's okay, Nick, I whispered, tears streaming down my own face. I've got you. We're going to let her rest. Together. It's the right thing to do.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. looks into his eyes, nodding slowly with tragic acceptance

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Okay, P. / Okay.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet hospital west-wing corridor. both turn to look back at the ICU double doors, ready to proceed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We turned together and walked back toward the ICU double doors, our hands clasped tightly, ready to face the hardest walk of our lives.

music_intent.style_description: Tense, rising cello and viola duet, accompanied by low, dramatic synthesizer swells and a slow, sorrowful piano melody
music_intent.mood_arc: Hallway Pacing → Nick's Panic → The Glass Window → Sibling Embrace

scenes.setting: Day 273 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - Saying Goodbye
scenes.summary: Day 273 - Milwaukee General Hospital (Interior) - Saying Goodbye — We gathered around her bed. Sionna stood near the doorway, a silent, comforting presence. The doctor and nurse stood at the foot of the bed, their faces grave.
