What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Quiet, sacred morning sunlight pouring through the ICU window, bathing Ma's bed in a peaceful, golden rectangle
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. stand on opposite sides of Ma's metal ICU bed, holding her cool hands, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We gathered around her bed. Sionna stood near the doorway, a silent, comforting presence. The doctor and nurse stood at the foot of the bed, their faces grave.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. stands quietly near the door, her hands folded, weeping softly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I held her left hand; Nick held her right. We looked at each other across her small, frail body, our hearts beating in a ragged, matching rhythm.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. reaches out to gently stroke Ma's silver hair, speaking in a whisper, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We're ready, I whispered to the doctor. He nodded slowly and reached for the dials on the respirator panel. The machine's steady hum suddenly stopped.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. leans down, kissing her pale cheek with pure love

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: The quiet was deafening. The thick plastic tube was removed, and for a second, her chest was completely still. I held my breath, waiting.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. adjusts a dialysis machine in the background, looking solemn, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then, she took a breath. A real, natural breath. It was slow, shallow, but it was all hers. Her chest rose, then fell with a soft, quiet sigh.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. stands over Ma, his hand pressing a dial on the respirator panel, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She looked so incredibly peaceful. The tension in her face, the flustered worries of a lifetime, just melted away. She looked beautiful in the dim light.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. reaches over to turn off the main switch of the mechanical ventilator, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She took another breath, even shallower, as if she was slowly drifting away into a deep, warm ocean. She was letting go, and we were letting her.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. watches the glowing screens of the monitor slowly turn blank and quiet, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick squeezed her hand. I love you, Ma, he whispered, his voice incredibly sweet and gentle. Thank you for everything. You can sleep now. We'll be okay.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. lies peaceful, her silver hair spread, her chest no longer rising artificially, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I leaned down and kissed her cheek. Goodbye, Ma, I sobbed. I'll write our story, I promise. I'll make sure everyone knows how much we loved you.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. takes a slow, shallow breath, her chest rising naturally one last time, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She took one final, tiny breath. It was barely a whisper. Her chest fell, and then... it didn't rise again. She was still. Completely still.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. exhales a final, soft sigh, her face completely tranquil and relaxed, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The green lines on the heart monitor began to flatten. The rhythmic beeping turned into a single, long, continuous tone, echoing in the quiet room.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. squeezes her hand, tears streaming down his face as he watches her go, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The nurse reached over and turned off the monitor's alarm. The sudden silence was absolute. It felt as if the entire world had stopped spinning.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. stares down, his shoulders shaking as he squeezes her other hand, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The doctor checked her eyes and her chest, then looked up at us. Time of death, 10:42 AM, he said softly. I am so sorry for your loss, boys.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. watches the heart rate monitor flatline with a quiet, continuous tone, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I let go of her hand and stepped around the bed. Nick met me half-way, and we fell into each other's arms, weeping with a shared, pure heartbreak.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. turns off the heart monitor's audio, bringing a sudden, heavy silence, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We had lost our mother, our only anchor, the woman who had bound our chaotic lives together. We were completely alone in the world now.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, light blue t-shirt, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. both bend over her body, weeping together in a shared embrace of grief, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But as we held each other, sobbing in the quiet room, I knew we still had each other. We were brothers. Nick and P. And that would have to be enough.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the quiet, sun-lit ICU room. stands over her, looking peaceful in the dim, quiet hospital room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked back at Ma's quiet face. She looked like she was sleeping, beautiful and free. The energy that had animated her was gone, back to the vast universe.

music_intent.style_description: Gentle, soaring orchestral strings with a soft acoustic guitar and a beautiful, mournful solo violin melody, fading into absolute silence
music_intent.mood_arc: Gathered Bedside → The Ventilator Stops → Natural Breath → The Final Sigh

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The apartment was exactly the same as we had left it. Her reading glasses were still on the side table; her slippers were tucked under the chair.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. looks toward Ma's empty floral armchair, bathed in a dusty shaft of sunlight, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But she wasn't there. The silence in the house was a physical thing. It pressed against my ears, heavy, thick, and suffocating.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. paces into the quiet kitchen, looking at Ma's empty coffee mug on the counter, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick walked past me without saying a single word. He went straight into his bedroom and shut the door behind him. The click of his lock was final.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. walks past P without a word, his face a dark, silent mask of pain, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stood in the kitchen, staring at Ma's blue coffee mug sitting in the sink, half-filled with cold, dried liquid from Saturday morning.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. enters his bedroom, slamming the wooden door shut with a heavy click, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It was a small, stupid thing, but it broke me. She had made coffee, expecting to drink it, expecting to live. And now she was gone.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. stands in the hallway, staring at the closed bedroom door with a look of worry, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna came up behind me. She took the cup out of the sink, washed it gently, and put it away. Then she made me a mug of hot chamomile tea.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. walks over to P, gently placing a warm mug of chamomile tea in his hands

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Sit down, P. / You need to rest. You haven't slept in thirty-six hours. Sit with me.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. sits at the dark wooden kitchen table, staring blankly down at the tea steam, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I sat at the wooden table, staring at the steam rising from the mug. I felt completely empty, as if my insides had been scooped out with a spoon.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. sits beside P, gently rubbing his back in a slow, comforting circle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Her absence was larger than her presence had ever been. She had filled this house with her flustered movements, her laundry, her worries. Now, just empty air.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. looks out the dark kitchen window reflecting the quiet night, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I leaned my head against the cool wood of the table, feeling the smooth grain against my temple. The wood felt real, solid. I needed something solid.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. closes his eyes, leaning his forehead against the cool table surface, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna sat next to me, her hand warm and soft on my shoulder, rubbing in slow, comforting circles. She didn't try to fill the silence with useless words.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. holds P's hand tightly, her light blue eyes filled with tears of empathy

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: She stayed there, a quiet, fierce presence, keeping the dark shadows of the empty house from swallowing me completely.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. stares at Ma's empty floral armchair, feeling the heavy silence of the house, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked toward the dark hallway leading to her bedroom. I half-expected to see her shuffle out in her pink housecoat, asking what we wanted for dinner.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. looks toward the dark hallway, the quietness of the apartment overwhelming, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But the hallway remained dark and silent. Ma was gone. There would be no more flustered questions, no more mass, no more lavender soap.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the quiet, shadow-filled apartment living room. lies on the sofa under a knitted blanket, staring blankly at the ceiling, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I lay down on the sofa, pulling Ma's knitted blanket over my shoulders. I stared up at the plaster ceiling, watching the car headlights sweep past the window.

--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual

laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

--- PAGE 31 ---
 “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife 
I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. 
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was 
dozing off in her bed. That was the weirdest thing ever. I still felt like it was real. Why would 
Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old 
people’s brain. Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled 
with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills 
for the week in
--- PAGE 32 ---
 Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, 
explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container. “See Ma, right here is every day 
of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for 
morning and night” 
 “Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container 
suspiciously. 
 “No worries Ma. I’m goin’ to be here to make sure everything is okay, and make sure 
you get the right pill at the right time.” 
 She looked up at me with those tired brown eyes of hers. “You’re such a good boy. You 
and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?” 
 I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of 
anything. That was a laugh. The only thing Nick did lately was criticize me for anything he could 
think of. Speak of the Devil, in walked Nick. His face was the usual beet red, eyes wild with 
some kind of inner rage. His hair all helter-skelter was framing his pox-marked face. I swear 
sometimes he looked like he came straight out of prison. Like he just broke loose and was 
looking for vengeance – a kind of vengeance you see in a war movie. 
 “What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he 
laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up. 
 “Knock it off man. Somebody has to make sure she stays on track. Where have you 
been?” I sounded accusingly. 
 “Don’t start giving 
--- PAGE 33 ---
 “I know, I know, just sayin’, it’s eight o’clock at night Man. Ma’s been home for like 
four hours.” 
 “All right, look” said Nick, “I’m not giving you hell. Just what is the deal around here 
anyway? Are you going to keep her from strokin’ out, or what?” He settled himself down in the 
overstuffed brown chair, kicking his big booted feet onto the footstool. 
 “Well, that is what I’m tryin’ to do.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Cold, gloomy, overcast daylight with a constant, gray rain misting the green hills of the cemetery

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The funeral was small and quiet. St. Patrick's was empty except for a few neighbors and the priest. Then we drove out to the cemetery in the pouring rain.

audio.speaker: Character_P

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick stood beside me. He had refused to wear a coat, standing in his dark suit, his reddish-brown hair getting soaked, staring at the coffin like a sentinel.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_M (elderly woman, silver hair, pale skin, white hospital gown) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. lies suspended on thick straps over a freshly dug grave in the wet grass, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The priest read the final prayers, his voice competing with the sound of the rain tapping on our umbrellas and the distant rumble of thunder.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. holds a single red rose, his fingers gripping the stem tightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ashes to ashes, dust to dust, he said, his hand making a sign of the cross over the polished wood. The words felt so incredibly old, so heavy.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. holds a small black prayer book, his lips moving in silent recitation, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick stepped forward. He had a single red rose in his large hand. He stood at the edge of the open grave, staring down into the dark, wet earth.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. stands in the background under umbrellas, their heads bowed respectfully, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He held the rose for a long, quiet moment, as if he was trying to think of something to say. But he didn't speak. He just let the rose drop.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. looks toward Nick, his heart breaking as he sees his brother's rigid posture, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I watched the rose fall, tumbling through the gray air to land on the center of the coffin lid. It looked so small, so bright against the dark wood.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. steps forward, his large hand trembling as he holds the red rose over the grave, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then the straps creaked. The metal lowering device groaned softly, and the coffin began its slow, final descent into the dark ground.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. drops the single red rose onto the polished wood of the coffin, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It sank slowly, disappearing inch by inch into the dark earth. It felt so incredibly permanent. This was the final door closing, once and for all.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. watches the rose land softly on the wood surface, raindrops splashing around it, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. presses a lever on the metal lowering device, and the straps begin to slide, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I put my arm around her, but my eyes were glued to the grave. I watched the wet dirt at the edge slide down, landing on the rose and the wood.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. watches the coffin slowly descend into the dark, wet earth of the grave, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick didn't move. He stood at the grave's edge, his hands shoved deep into his suit pockets, watching the coffin descend until it was completely out of sight.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. leans her head against P's shoulder, weeping softly under the rain, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He looked like he wanted to jump in after her, or fight the dirt, or fight the rain. But there was nothing to fight. The silence of the earth had won.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. weeps silently, his shoulders shaking under the dark grey sweater, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: When the grave was filled, the priest shook our hands and left. The few neighbors walked back to their cars, leaving the three of us alone under the gray sky.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. stares down into the empty dark hole, his face a cold, unmoving stone, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked at Nick. His suit was soaked, his hair plastered to his forehead. Come on, Nick, I said gently. Let's go home. He didn't answer, just turned.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in the wet, rain-swept cemetery graveside. all stand in a quiet circle under the falling rain, bidding a final farewell, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He walked back to his Pontiac, his heavy steps slow and methodical. We got into the Civic, and followed him back to the empty, quiet apartment.

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. stands near the hallway, her face tight with anxiety as she watches the brothers, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You're so quiet, P, he sneered suddenly, his voice loud and slurred. The writer has nothing to say? No big words for Ma's funeral? No smart stories?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. takes a swig from a bottle of whiskey, his eyes wild and bloodshot, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I didn't answer. I just sat at the kitchen table, watching him. This wasn't the Nick who held Ma's hand. This was the wild, dangerous brawler.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. stands up, pointing an angry finger at P, his breathing ragged

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: You think you're better than me, don't you? With your college classes and your pretty girlfriend!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. stands up to face him, his own face tight with rising anger, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stood up to face him. I was tired of being afraid of him. I was tired of his chaos. Shut up, Nick, I said, my voice cold. Just shut the hell up.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. slams his whiskey bottle down on the side table, shattering a ceramic lamp; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: He roared, a wild, animal sound, and slammed his whiskey bottle down. It missed the table, hitting Ma's favorite ceramic lamp and shattering it into a thousand pieces.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. flinches at the sudden crash of the lamp, stepping back toward the kitchen, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna gasped, stepping back into the hallway, her face white with terror. She wanted to help, but she knew that entering this arena was dangerous.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. shouts at P, his face contorted in a mask of grief and fury

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Why didn't you save her, P? / You're the smart one! Why didn't you do something? Why did you let her die?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. shouts back, his hands clenched at his sides, refusing to back down, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I felt a wave of hot, blinding fury crash through my chest. How dare he? How dare he blame me? I did everything, Nick! I took care of her while you were out fighting!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. glares at P, his chest heaving under his black leather jacket, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I screamed back, my voice cracking with the strain. I was the one who was here! You were never here, Nick! You only showed up when you wanted to play hero!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. speaks with trembling anger, laying bare all his old childhood hurts

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick's face turned purple. He lunged across the room. Before I could move, his large hand shot out, grabbing my collar and yanking me forward with massive force.

story_beats.visual_event: Messy dark apartment living room. Face-to-face: Character_N (muscular, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket) steps toward Character_P (thin frame), yanks P collar close — eye to eye, whiskey-breath distance. Profile/side two-shot, progressive yank. Lips closed.

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He pulled me so close I could smell the hot whiskey on his breath. His eyes were wide, bloodshot, and completely insane with grief and rage.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. reaches out to try and intervene, her voice filled with panic

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Nick, stop! / Let him go! You're hurting him! Nick, please!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. shoves her back gently but firmly with his other arm, shouting in her face

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Leave us alone, Sionna! / This is between me and my brother!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. holds P's collar tightly, shaking him with a terrifying, wild energy

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: He turned back to me, shaking me by my collar, his teeth bared in a snarl. I should have broken your neck years ago, P! You're a coward! A useless coward!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. stares into his brother's wild eyes, refusing to show fear despite the threat, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I didn't flinch. I stared straight into his bloodshot eyes, my hands gripping his wrists, my own face contorted in a matching mask of fury.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair slicked, black leather jacket, jeans) in the messy, shadow-filled apartment living room. glare at each other, the tension in the room reaching an explosive peak, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Do it then, Nick! I screamed in his face. Do it! Hit me! Prove that you're just a monster! That's all you know how to do anyway!

music_intent.style_description: Aggressive, distorted electric guitar chords with a rapid, chaotic drum beat and a screaming synthesizer drone
music_intent.mood_arc: Tension Builds → Whiskey Rage → Ceramic Shatters → Grief and Fury

scenes.setting: Night 274 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - The Fistfight
scenes.summary: Night 274 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - The Fistfight — I didn't wait for him to hit me. I reached out and shoved him back with all my strength, breaking his grip on my collar. He stumbled back against the table.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

--- PAGE 31 ---
 “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife 
I have ever imagined into my chest. I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me. 
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was 
dozing off in her bed. That was the weirdest thing ever. I still felt like it was real. Why would 
Nick want to kill me? Me? His little brother. I felt betrayed and so confused. It took me a good 
fifteen minutes to come out of it and tell myself it was only a dream. Still, for the rest of the day I 
had an awful feeling. It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be 
the worst betrayal of all. 
 I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set 
up to come home. The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew 
how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications. She also had some pills 
called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain. It didn’t sound like they were as 
worried about a heart attack as they were about her stroking out from her high blood pressure. 
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old 
people’s brain. Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled 
with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday. This way, we could put all of Ma’s pills 
for the week in
--- PAGE 32 ---
 Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, 
explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container. “See Ma, right here is every day 
of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for 
morning and night” 
 “Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container 
suspiciously. 
 “No worries Ma. I’m goin’ to be here to make sure everything is okay, and make sure 
you get the right pill at the right time.” 
 She looked up at me with those tired brown eyes of hers. “You’re such a good boy. You 
and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?” 
 I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of 
anything. That was a laugh. The only thing Nick did lately was criticize me for anything he could 
think of. Speak of the Devil, in walked Nick. His face was the usual beet red, eyes wild with 
some kind of inner rage. His hair all helter-skelter was framing his pox-marked face. I swear 
sometimes he looked like he came straight out of prison. Like he just broke loose and was 
looking for vengeance – a kind of vengeance you see in a war movie. 
 “What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he 
laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up. 
 “Knock it off man. Somebody has to make sure she stays on track. Where have you 
been?” I sounded accusingly. 
 “Don’t start giving 
--- PAGE 33 ---
 “I know, I know, just sayin’, it’s eight o’clock at night Man. Ma’s been home for like 
four hours.” 
 “All right, look” said Nick, “I’m not giving you hell. Just what is the deal around here 
anyway? Are you going to keep her from strokin’ out, or what?” He settled himself down in the 
overstuffed brown chair, kicking his big booted feet onto the footstool. 
 “Well, that is what I’m tryin’ to do.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Dim, dramatic shadows stretching across the floorboards, illuminated by the single unshaded yellow bulb hanging overhead
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. reaches out and shoves Nick back with all his strength, breaking his collar hold, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I didn't wait for him to hit me. I reached out and shoved him back with all my strength, breaking his grip on my collar. He stumbled back against the table.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. stumbles back against the dining table, his face a wild mask of shock and fury, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick looked at me, completely shocked. Nobody shoved Nick. Nobody stood up to him. His face contorted in a wild, terrifying mask of pure fury.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. lunges forward, throwing a wild, heavy punch toward P's head, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He lunged, throwing a massive, wild punch. I ducked, feeling the wind of his fist pass my ear, and threw a punch of my own, straight from the shoulder.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. ducks his head, the heavy fist grazing his ear, and throws a wild punch back, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My fist hit his jaw with a sharp, satisfying crack. His head snapped sideways. But instead of falling, he roared and tackled me around the waist.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. feels P's fist hit his jaw with a sharp crack, his head snapping sideways, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We crashed into the dark wooden dining table. The old wood splintered with a deafening bang, and we both tumbled to the floor in a heap of limbs and glass.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. tackle each other, crashing heavily into the dark wooden dining table, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We rolled over and over on the floorboards, grabbing, scratching, clawing. We weren't brothers anymore. We were two animals fighting for survival.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. groan as they tumble to the floorboards amidst splintered wood and broken glass, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna was screaming in the background, her voice a distant, panic-filled siren. But we didn't hear her. We were locked in our own private war.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, black blouse, jeans, light blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. stands in the doorway, her hands over her mouth, screaming in terror and panic

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Nick rolled on top of me, Pinning my shoulders to the floor with his massive weight. He raised his heavy fist, ready to smash my face in.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. grapple on the floor, rolling over and over in a chaotic tangle of limbs, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stared up at his fist, my heart pounding, refusing to close my eyes. Hit me, Nick! I choked out. Hit me! But he didn't. He froze.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. grabs P's throat, Pinning him down onto the floor, his face red with effort, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: His fist remained raised in the air, trembling. His face contorted, not with anger, but with an overwhelming, unbearable wave of pure pain.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. struggles violently beneath him, scratching and clawing at Nick's large arms, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: His mouth opened in a silent, silent scream of agony. He looked at his fist, then at my face, and his large shoulders suddenly collapsed, shaking violently.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. stops suddenly, his heavy fist raised in the air, his face contorting in pain, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He lowered his fist. He let go of my collar. And then, he fell forward, his forehead resting against my chest, and he wept.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. stares up at him, panting heavily, his face wet with tears and sweat

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: He sobbed with a raw, gasping heartbreak that shook his entire frame. He wasn't the brawler anymore. He was just a boy who had lost his mother.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. slowly lowers his raised fist, his shoulders beginning to shake violently, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I lay there on the cold floorboards, panting heavily, my chest rising and falling beneath his weight. The anger inside of me just evaporated, leaving only sorrow.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. bends his head down, resting his forehead against P's chest as he bursts into tears, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person; show clear progressive motion of the main action, not a frozen pose

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I reached up and wrapped my arms around his broad shoulders, holding him tightly against me. It's okay, Nick, I whispered, my own tears falling fast.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. wraps his arms around Nick's broad shoulders, clutching his brother tightly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I've got you, big bro. I'm right here. We're going to be okay. He gripped my shirt, burying his face in my chest, crying like a child.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. both lie on the floorboards in the wreckage of the table, weeping in a shared embrace, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We lay there in the wreckage of our dining table, surrounded by splintered wood and broken glass, two brothers holding each other in the dark, quiet room.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the messy, damaged apartment living room. hold each other tightly, the physical fight completely resolved in shared grief, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The war was over. The anger was gone. All that was left was the shared, pure grief of our loss, and the silent, unbreakable bond of our blood.

music_intent.style_description: Violent, driving rock beat with screaming guitar feedback, transitioning into a slow, emotional solo cello and tender piano notes
music_intent.mood_arc: The First Blow → Table Splinters → Floorboard Grapple → Fist Raised

scenes.setting: Day 275 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Morning Light
scenes.summary: Day 275 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - Morning Light — The next morning, the apartment was bathed in soft, beautiful spring daylight. The storm had passed, leaving the air outside clean, fresh, and quiet.

 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if

 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and

because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a
