story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. stands near the brick fireplace, a warm fire crackling in the grate, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I cleared my throat, my hands trembling slightly as I held the leather cover. Reading your work to someone is like standing naked in the rain.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. opens his leather notebook, clearing his throat with a serious expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I started reading. I read about the chameleons on the living room rug, the cold Wisconsin winter, the bar fights, and Ma's lavender soap.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. reads aloud from his notebook, his voice rich and emotionally expressive, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: My voice was shaky at first, but as I got into the rhythm of the words, I felt the brawler's energy flow through me, strong, proud, and honest.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. watches P read, her light blue eyes shimmering with rising tears, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna didn't say a single word. She just sat beside me, her light blue eyes wide, staring at my face, listening to every syllable.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. points to a handwritten line on the paper, explaining with a small smile, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I saw a tear form in the corner of her eye, glistening under the firelight, then slide down her cheek as I read about Ma's final mass with Nick.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. nods in deep appreciation, a soft tear spilling down her pale cheek, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I read about the Pontiac's roar, the smell of wet pine, and the silent, unbreakable bond of our blood. The words felt alive, breathing in the room.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. continues reading, his hand steady as he speaks the final paragraphs of a chapter

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I finished the chapter. I read the final paragraph, my voice dropping to a whisper, and then... I closed the notebook. The click was loud in the silence.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. reaches out to gently touch P's knee, her face filled with profound emotion

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: I sat there, my heart pounding, looking down at the leather cover, terrified of what she would say. The silence stretched for three seconds, four, five.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. closes his leather notebook with a soft, satisfying click of the cover, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then Sionna didn't speak. She just lunged across the cushions and wrapped her arms around my neck, holding me with a tight, fierce strength.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. stares at the notebook cover, his heart beating fast with creative vulnerability, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She was sobbing, but it was a good, cathartic cry. P, she whispered in my ear, her voice cracking. It is absolutely magnificent. It is so real.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. steps closer to P on the sofa, wrapping her arms tightly around his neck

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: Ma would have loved it so much. And Nick... you've made him a king, P. You've written him exactly as he is – a warrior who loved us.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. hugs Sionna back, burying his face in her short brown hair with a sigh of relief, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I felt a massive wave of joy and peace wash through my soul. I wrapped my arms around her, holding her close, smelling her sweet jasmine hair.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. wipes a tear from her cheek, smiling with deep love and mutual respect, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I knew right then that I had done the right thing. I had kept my promise to Ma. I had built the blueprint of our lives, one word at a time.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. holds her hand, both staring into the crackling orange embers of the fireplace, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We sat in the firelight, watching the orange embers glow and fade, holding hands under the warm blanket, completely at peace with the world.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in Sionna's cozy fireside duplex living room. sit together on the sofa in the warm, peaceful glow of the living room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The nightmare had officially turned into art. The healing had begun. I was ready to write the next chapter, and the next, until it was done.

music_intent.style_description: Sweet, emotional acoustic guitar strumming with gentle solo piano chords, soaring cello, and a beautiful, weeping violin melody
music_intent.mood_arc: Floral Sofa → Reading Aloud → Firelight Tears → Sionna's Sob

scenes.setting: Day 360 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - One Year Later
scenes.summary: Day 360 - Narrator's Apartment (Interior) - One Year Later — One year later, the apartment was empty. The lease was up, and Sionna and I were moving into a beautiful little house near the lake. We were packing boxes.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Bright, warm afternoon sunlight flooding through empty window panes, casting long, clean rectangular shapes on bare wood floorboards
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. stands in his empty apartment living room, carrying a taped cardboard packing box, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: One year later, the apartment was empty. The lease was up, and Sionna and I were moving into a beautiful little house near the lake. We were packing boxes.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. places the taped cardboard box onto a stack of three other boxes near the door, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The rooms looked so small without Ma's furniture, Ma's laundry, and Ma's worries. But they didn't feel dark anymore. They felt clean, fresh, and quiet.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. stands beside a taped box, holding a dark plastic roll of packaging tape, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I had finished the manuscript of 'Nick and Me' last week. My professor had sent it to an editor in New York. The blueprint was built, once and for all.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. looks around the clean, empty room with a quiet and reflective smile, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna handed me the tape dispenser. She was wearing her yellow sun hat and green tank top, her short brown hair messy from packing. She looked beautiful.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. hands P a dark plastic roll of packaging tape with a bright, happy smile

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: We're almost done. / Just one more trip in the Civic.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. stands beside him in her green tank top, looking excited and happy, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Suddenly, the old rotary phone in the hallway rang. The sound echoed loudly against the bare plaster walls, shocking both of us. It was loud.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. both look toward Ma's empty floral armchair, which is now gone, leaving a clean space, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I walked down the hall and lifted the receiver. Hello? I said. There was a massive, crackling static on the line, like a call from outer space.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. stares at the clean wood floorboards where Ma's chair used to stand, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Then I heard the roar of wind, a high-pitched engine, and a deep, gravelly laugh. Hey, kid! a voice roared. You there? It was Nick.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. walks down the dark hallway as a telephone rings, his footsteps echoing on the wood, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Nick! I yelled, my heart doing a giant leap of pure joy. Where the hell are you? He laughed, the sound loud, rough, and completely happy.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. reaches out a hand to lift the black rotary phone receiver from its wall cradle, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I'm in Alaska, P! he shouted over the static wind. Working on an oil rig near Prudhoe Bay. It's freezing, it's brutal, and I've already won three fights!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. holds the receiver to his ear, his eyes widening in sudden, joyful surprise, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I chuckled, running a hand through my hair. Of course you did, Nick. Some things never change. You still the meanest son of a bitch up there?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. smiles with pure joy as he hears Nick's voice on the static line, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You bet! he roared. But hey, kid... I got a letter from Sterling's office. He said your book is getting published. A real publisher in New York!

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. stands in his grey work shirt under a bright blue sky, holding a radio phone transmitter

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: Is that true, P? Are you a real writer now?

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. looks out at massive snowy mountains in the background, a huge smile on his face

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I knew it, P.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. laughs, his muscular chest expanding under his grey plaid work shirt

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I always knew you'd do it. Ma knew it too. I'm so proud of you, big bro. You're the smart one. You made us proud. You did your job.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. speaks with rough, brotherly affection and immense pride, gesturing with his hand

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Thanks, Nick, I whispered, tears of joy streaming down my cheeks. I couldn't have written a word without you. You're the hero of the story, Nick.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. nods with tears of joy in his eyes, leaning against the hallway wall happily, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He laughed, his brawler's bravado returning. You tell them that! I've gotta go, kid. The foreman is yelling. Look after Sionna! Love you, bro.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the empty apartment hallway and bedroom. hangs up the receiver with a soft click, looking back at Sionna with pure determination, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I love you too, Nick, I said. He hung up, and the dial tone hummed in the quiet. I put the receiver back. I looked at Sionna. We were ready to go.

music_intent.style_description: Uplifting, driving acoustic guitar strumming with bright piano chords, hopeful violin, and a joyous glockenspiel melody
music_intent.mood_arc: Packing Boxes → Taping Room → Phone Ringing → Nick's Voice

scenes.setting: Day 360 - Lake Mendota (Exterior) - Golden Hour
scenes.summary: Day 360 - Lake Mendota (Exterior) - Golden Hour — Later that evening, Sionna and I walked along the shore of Lake Michigan. The sun was setting, casting a gorgeous golden path of light across the water.

 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

8

Geez, Nick is pissed off. I get home and he's stomping around the place getting Ma all 
upset about something. Seems I haven't been around a lot, and he's mad at me. He gives me this 
look like he wants to kill me or something. I don't know what to say. I'm trying not to cringe, but 
he's so mad. 
 “What’s going on Nick?” I ask, trying to keep as calm as possible. I don’t want to look 
afraid in front of him. It’s just not cool. 
 “Ma missed her doctor’s appointment yesterday, you ass. Where the hell were you? 
Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

audio.speaker: Character_P

audio.speaker: Character_P

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. looks at Sionna, his thin frame backlit by the gorgeous orange sunset glow, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. squeezes her warm hand, his face filled with deep love and absolute peace, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I'm not analyzing, I said softly. I'm just appreciating. I was thinking about what we talked about on her living room floor months ago. About energy.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. stops walking, turning to face P on the gravel path with a soft, proud smile, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: God is just energy, she had said. It's in all of us, it existed before time, and it goes on forever. Standing here, I finally understood it.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. takes a deep, peaceful breath, smelling the fresh water and wet pine trees, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Ma's energy wasn't gone. It was in the trees, in the water, in the golden sun, and in the words I had written in my notebook. She was everywhere.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. holds both of P's hands, looking into his eyes with pure adoration and wisdom

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: And Nick's energy... it was up in Alaska, fighting the cold, brawling with the wind, living with a fierce, wild independent freedom. He was alive.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. looks down at their joined hands, feeling the warm, strong energy between them, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Whatever energy bound the three of us together, it was the strongest force in my world. It had survived the storm. It would survive forever.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. speaks with gentle, quiet confidence, her light blue eyes reflecting the sun

audio.speaker: Character_P

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. nods slowly, a soft, genuine smile on his face as he looks across the lake, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We're going to be okay, Sionna, I whispered, looking into her light blue eyes. We're going to build a beautiful life. She smiled, kissing my cheek.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. leans forward, sharing a beautiful, sweet kiss with P on the gravel pathway, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I know we are, P, she said. You've written the past. Now we get to write the future. Together. The gravel pathway opened up ahead of us.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. hugs Sionna, holding her close as the golden sun starts to touch the horizon, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We walked on, holding hands, our steps matching in a slow, rhythmic, peaceful stride under the glorious Wisconsin sky. We were home.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. both look out at the endless water, watching the golden path of light expand, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (thin frame, dark grey sweater, jeans, blue eyes), Character_S (short brown hair, green tank top, jeans, light blue eyes) in the gravel pathway beside the vast calm lake shore. stands in a silent, beautiful embrace under the glorious Wisconsin sunset sky, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

scenes.story_day: Present Day - P's Writing Studio (Interior) - Finishing the Manuscript
scenes.summary: Present Day - P's Writing Studio (Interior) - Finishing the Manuscript — PRESENT-DAY FRAMING DEVICE: Ten years have passed. I am sitting in my writing studio, the walls lined with mahogany bookshelves and framed book covers.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. stares at the glowing computer screen, his face serious, hands typing rapidly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.dialogue: I am typing the final lines of the manuscript. The computer screen glows in the quiet room, reflecting the blue of my eyes. The keyboard clicks fast.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. types the final words on the digital page with a focused, emotional expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I didn't write it in a leather notebook this time. I wrote it on a screen, but the energy of the words was exactly the same – raw, honest, and alive.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. types: 'The End. For Ma, and for Nick.', his fingers pressing keys with resolve, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I typed the final letters: 'The End. For Ma, and for Nick.' My fingers lingered on the keys, feeling the heavy, beautiful finality of the moment.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. reaches out to click a digital 'Save' icon on the screen with a deep breath of relief, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I reached out and clicked 'Save'. The progress bar loaded, completed, and disappeared. It was done. The book was finished. I took a deep, shaking breath.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. watches the progress bar load and complete, a soft smile appearing on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I felt a massive weight lift from my shoulders, a weight I had carried since that rainy night in Sturgeon Bay ten years ago. I had kept my promise.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. stands in the writing studio doorway in her white knit sweater, looking beautiful, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Sionna walked into the studio. She was wearing a white knit sweater, her short brown hair styled beautifully, her light blue eyes full of pride.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. carries a steaming ceramic mug of jasmine tea, walking slowly toward P's desk

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: She carried a steaming ceramic mug of jasmine tea. She placed it gently beside my keyboard and leaned down, wrapping her arms around my neck.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. places the steaming mug gently beside his computer keyboard with a warm smile

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: You did it, P. / You finished the story.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. leans down, wrapping her arms around P's shoulders with a sweet embrace, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked up at her, my eyes wet with a sudden, warm tear of pure joy. I couldn't have done it without you, Sionna, I whispered. You saved my life.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. kisses P's temple with pure love, her short brown hair brushing his cheek

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: No, P. You saved your own life. With your words. I'm so proud of you.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. looks up at Sionna, his eyes filled with tears of deep gratitude and joy, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: She looked at the computer screen, reading the final paragraph. Her smile was soft, proud, and incredibly emotional. P, this is going to change things.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. strokes his hair with her warm, soft hand, looking at his computer screen, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I stood up and wrapped my arms around her waist, pulling her close. We stood in the center of the studio, bathed in the soft afternoon daylight.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. reads the final words on the screen, a proud, emotional smile on her face

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: I had spent ten years analyzing, writing, and reliving the nightmare. But standing here, holding the woman I loved, I knew the nightmare was gone.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. holds her hand tightly, leaning his head back against her chest with a sigh, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: All that was left was the art. The legacy of Ma's love and Nick's shield was immortalized on those digital pages, safe from time and space.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. stands up from his desk chair, standing face to face with Sionna in the sun-lit room, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: We looked out the studio window. Below, the trees were green under the summer sun, and the lake shimmers on the horizon. The world was beautiful.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. hugs Sionna tightly, holding her close under the beautiful afternoon daylight, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: The energy that bound us together was still the strongest force in my world. It had shaped my past, and it would guide my future. I was completely at peace.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in the modern, sun-lit writing studio desk. both look out the studio window, a sense of absolute triumph and peace in the air, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I took a sip of my jasmine tea, my hand steady, my eyes clear. The brawler's brother was a writer now. The story of Nick and P was ready for the world.

music_intent.style_description: Contemplative, soaring solo piano with warm ambient synthesizer pads, celestial bells, and a beautiful, proud violin harmony
music_intent.mood_arc: Modern Studio → Keyboard Clicks → Final Paragraph → Saving File

scenes.setting: Present Day - P's Writing Studio (Interior) - The Legacy of Nick
scenes.story_day: Present Day - P's Writing Studio (Interior) - The Legacy of Nick
scenes.summary: Present Day - P's Writing Studio (Interior) - The Legacy of Nick — You don't get to choose your brother. They are just handed to you by fate, a chaotic, brawling package of blood, bones, and shared history.

source_excerpts.excerpt:
--- PAGE 46 ---
 “No.” she said calmly. “Why do you let other people bother you like that? You know 
they’re just kids, and there’s nothing funny about what we’re doing, so, just ignore them.” 
 “Well I know that logically, but it still pisses me off” I didn’t know how to describe my 
symptoms of inner rage. 
 “Well, I’ll tell you something. Only when we become masters of ourselves, can we really 
deal with the world. What I mean is, the world is full of stupid, violent, seemingly useless 
people. But you aren’t stupid, violent, or useless. You know more than they do. You can see that 
your feelings are not logical, and you can deal with moving on. You have a purpose. We have to 
live with all kinds of people that aren’t as advanced as we are spiritually.” 
 “Do you really think we are more advanced spiritually? How do you know they aren’t 
yet, but might become like Buddha or something?” 
 “So if they do become like Buddha, we should definitely leave them alone, so they can 
follow that path. I mean, if you think one of those boys is going to become the next spiritual 
master, then you should be glad you got a glimpse of what they used to be like!” She just started 
laughing, and so did I. Thinking about one of those numbskulls becoming enlightened made me 
laugh. 
 “You’re really something, you know that?” 
 “You’re really something too.” She looked up at me with her ocean eyes and I leaned 
down and kissed her. That kiss was like slow motion. It was like there was no one else around, 
and
--- PAGE 47 ---
 “That is one of our missions, I think.” She looked pensive. “We have to learn how to 
speak it to everyone, even strangers, and even people we don’t particularly like.” 
 “That’s going to be hard” I said. 
 “I know. That is the meaning of “loving kindness”. That is the ability to open our hearts 
and accept everyone, even those kids on the corner laughing at us. It might take my whole life, 
but I am bound and determined to find that inside of me and bring it out.” 
 “Let me know if you do, because if my girlfriend is loving everyone, I think I’m going to 
need a new one!” I started laughing. 
 “I could never love anyone like I love you.” That was it. That was what had not been 
said, and now it was said. I would remember the way she looked at me for the rest of my life. My 
girlfriend told me she loved me, and it was the most sincere statement I have ever heard. It really 
came from her heart. 
 “I could never love anyone the way I love you either.” We just smiled at each other and 
kept on walking.

--- PAGE 48 ---
 4

Nick was laughing so hard, I thought he might fall off his couch. He had smoke 
sputtering out of the corner of his mouth, and his face was beet red as usual. At that moment I 
wondered why I had even taken the time to come over to his apartment. 
 “Come on, Bro. You haven’t been to my place for ages.” His argument finally persuaded 
me to ride over to his place and sit around with him and a couple old friends before heading to 
Joe’s to play pool and hassle the waitresses. This was the usual scene: Nick invites people over, 
Nick gets hammered, and Nick has an audience to tell his stories to. Boring. I’ve heard all this 
shit about a million times. 
 “Man,” Bob said holding the ceramic pipe and blowing smoke, “I don’t know about you 
guys, but I’m needin’ some new action.

--- PAGE 41 ---
 “So, what’s up with you?” he said, pressing his lips into a strange pursed curl. 
 “Ah, not much. Just working and thinking about going to school” I said. I was expecting 
a huge knee-slapping laugh from Nick, but he just stared at me silently contemplating what I had 
just said. 
 “Going to school? How the hell are you going to pay for that? And what for?” His look 
displayed an incomprehensible expression, as if he had just heard Ma was pregnant or 
something. 
 “Well, I decided that there’s a lot of good jobs out there, but I have to have a degree to do 
anything worth doing, so I’m going to the community college and getting a bachelor’s degree in 
business. Maybe I can manage a restaurant or someplace like that.” I waited for his arguments. 
 “How the hell will you pay for it? How the hell are you going to have time to watch Ma if 
your screwing around with those pencil-pushers?” His eyes looked like they were set on fire. 
 “Sionna told me I could qualify for a low-interest student loan. I checked it all out, and 
applied for it, and they gave me the money. I mean, really, community college isn’t that much 
compared to other places.” 
 Nick cut me short. “Whoa, whoa, whoa dude. You have really done it now. I can’t 
believe you are letting this chick tell you what to do. It’s not like she’s gonna be around to help 
pay this shit back! That’s all on your head man. I can tell you one thing, it sure ain’t gonna be me 
helping your sorry ass out when you finish – IF you fini
--- PAGE 42 ---
want to work in a pizza place my whole life. Second, I don’t do everything Sionna tells me, this 
was my idea. And lastly, it would really mean a lot to me if you could just step up and be on my 
side for once, you know? You are my brother, man.” I was trying not to lose my cool composure, 
because we really needed to work things out. I needed him to work with me on this. 
 “Be on your side? Be on your side?” he said with an elevating voice. 
 “Man, do you actually remember your own life? How many times have I saved your ass? 
I mean really, can you keep count?” 
 Ma was starting to notice there was something unusual going on. She looked up with a 
confused, creased forehead. She started nodding her head side to side, as if to say, “Please don’t 
argue boys.” Slowly, she returned her eyes back to the television screen and folded her hands in 
her lap. 
 “I know, Nick, I know. I’m not saying that. What I mean is, I really need my family to 
support me on this. It’s going to be really hard, and take a lot of time. I will be here for Ma. 
Classes aren’t all day. I have to be in and out of here. I’ll make sure she’s okay.” 
 Nick looked pensive. “Do you remember that time in high school when Donny Amstel 
started harassing you? He was pushing you in the locker room after gym class and calling here 
hanging up, and threatening to steal you bike? Do you remember all that?” 
 “Yeah, sure, I do remember that. You told me you would take care of it, and then, all of a 
sudden, he just 
--- PAGE 43 ---
my body in his front door, so he couldn’t close it. Oh God man, he looked like he was about to 
shit his pants.” Nick started giggling like a little kid. “Then – then, oh man. Then, I told him if he 
ever threatened you like that again he would be killed. I told him our family had mafia 
connections. And he believed me! He was so scared, I couldn’t believe he actually believed me. 
What a dumbass!

1.

It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week 
before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property. I 
was almost out of fourth grade. Me and Bobby Swenson were laughing our asses off, folded over 
at the waist, grabbing our sides before they split out. Jennifer Bales and Sue Something-or-other 
were looking at us with stern faces and shaking their heads. 
 “You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” 
Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice. She was like a forty year old stuck in the body of a nine 
year old. Her brown braids ended halfway down her back with the flail of red bows that bounced 
off her when she walked. She and Sue walked toward the building, probably on the way to report 
our goofy behavior to Mrs. Stark. 
 “You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out 
through the raucous laughter. 
 “No, YOU have to learn some responsibility!” Bobby spit out, his face red and dribble 
running out of his mouth from the uncontrolled giggling. 
 “Learn some responsibility, learn some responsibility!” we cried in unison, mocking Mrs. 
Stark’s earlier comment in the classroom directed towards us both. We had been sending notes 
under the desk to one another, putting pencil eraser tips up our noses, and acting like 
tyrannosaurus rex monsters coming to life. Generally the type of t
--- PAGE 36 ---
school year when we got tired of long division and trying to write paragraphs with three parts. It 
was total relief. Total uncontrollable joy bubbling up from the far reaches inside, telling the 
world “we’re almost free! Hee hee!” 
 Apparently Mrs. Stark didn’t see it that way. We had been getting on her bad side for the 
last week or so. She was at the end of her rope. Ma got a call from her the other night, and told 
me if I didn’t straighten out and “fly right” I would be punished. She would take my bike away 
for a week. Crap, I didn’t want that to happen. But, I just couldn’t help myself today. Everything 
Bobby and I saw, or heard, was absolutely hilarious. I saw Nick from the corner of my eye. He 
was walking toward us; hair in a devilish mess. “What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked 
looking around for a clue. 
 Bobby just looked at me nervously, seemingly muted by Nick’s presence. Nick was a big 
bad-assed sixth-grader. He was at the top of the totem pole in our school. Not only was he in the 
senior class, he was tall, and big, and highly respected for his bravery in every situation. Since 
Bobby wasn’t going to answer, I said, “Oh, just the usual crap from Mrs. Stark. She thinks we 
need to learn some responsibility”. The last two words came out slow with a muffled sputter 
before I burst into laughter again, my face red, and tears starting to form in the corners of my 
eyes. Nick slapped me across the shoulder. “Little Bro, you better start listening, man.
--- PAGE 37 ---
Mr. Pauls was the principal, and he sure wasn’t a “pal”. He was kind of a big prick. He didn’t 
like getting into it with arguing kids, and he really didn’t like getting into it with disrespectful 
kids and their parents. 
 “Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby 
were just goofing around. You should see them around my house. It never ends. I’ll talk to him 
and Ma will talk to him. He’ll never act up in class again. I guarantee it.” 
 Mrs.

scenes.lighting_continuity_token: Warm, peaceful golden afternoon sun filtering through the window, bathing P's desk in a tranquil, glowing rectangular beam
spoiler_constraints: No name tags / personal names on clothing for Character_P (withheld_until_reveal)

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. turns his chair to look directly into the camera lens with a calm, confident expression, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: You don't get to choose your brother. They are just handed to you by fate, a chaotic, brawling package of blood, bones, and shared history.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. rests his hands on his desk armchair, speaking with deep philosophical warmth, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: For twenty years, I thought Nick was my nightmare. I thought his fights, his fast driving, and his chameleons were just traps to drag me down.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. looks down at his computer screen, a soft and reflective smile on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I was always trying to analyze him, trying to fit him into some neat, logical box. But you can't put a Viking hero in a box. It doesn't work.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. looks back up at the camera, his blue eyes bright with mature wisdom, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: It took Ma's passing and a quiet porch in Milwaukee for me to finally see the truth. Nick wasn't my nightmare. He was my shield.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. gestures with his hands as he explains the profound nature of sibling bonds, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He brawled so I didn't have to. He took the bruises, the broken glass, and the police records, all to keep my hands clean and my mind free.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. stands beside his desk, looking at P with a proud and supportive expression

audio.speaker: Character_S
audio.dialogue: He carried the weight of our survival on his broad shoulders, carrying bags of stolen wood and cheese, so I could study under a desk lamp.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. smiles back at Sionna, his face filled with deep, quiet gratitude, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: He believed in my beautiful brain when I had nothing but fear. He made me a writer before I ever wrote a single word of 'Nick and Me'.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. looks at the camera again, his voice rich and steady in deep narration, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: And that is his legacy. He didn't build bridges, he didn't write books, but he built me. He gave me the space to find my own purpose.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. stands in his grey work shirt, looking brave and independent in Alaska

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: I don't know when I'll see him again. Alaska is vast, and he's got a lot of cold wind to fight before his energy slows down. He's on his path.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. laughs in Alaska, his muscular frame backlit by the bright blue sky, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: But I know he's happy. I can hear it in his laugh, loud, rough, and free under the snowy mountains. He's doing his job. He's being Nick.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, grey work shirt, jeans) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. waves his hand with rough affection from the Alaska oil rig deck

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: We are still bound together by that same ancient energy Ma gave us, the strongest force in our world. It doesn't care about time or space.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. stands under the rainy cemetery sky, a strong sentinel in a dark suit

audio.speaker: Character_N
audio.dialogue: It existed before we were born, and it will go on forever. It's in the ink on my page, the static on the phone, and the sun on Lake Michigan.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_N (muscular build, reddish-brown hair, dark funeral suit, white shirt) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. holds a single red rose over the grave, his face tight with silent love, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I look at my finished book, and I don't feel grief anymore. I feel a deep, burning gratitude. I am Ma's boy. I am Nick's little brother.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. looks back at the camera in the studio, a peaceful smile on his face, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: And I am a writer. The nightmare is over, the blueprint is complete, and the story is safe. We survived, once and for all.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes), Character_S (older, short brown hair, white knit sweater, light blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. holds Character_S's hand, leaning against the mahogany bookcase, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I look at Sionna, my anchor, my keeper, the woman who opened the blinds and showed me the light. She is my present, and my future.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. looks out the window at the sunny garden, feeling the flow of energy, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: And Nick... Nick is my history. My warrior. My brother. I smile, looking into the camera lens with absolute, unshakable conviction.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in P's sun-lit, mahogany-walled writing studio. stands quiet and resolved in the warm light, ready for his next chapter, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Thank you, Nick, I say in my mind. Thank you for the shield. I'll take it from here. The camera slowly pans toward the lakeside window pane.

music_intent.style_description: Soaring, beautiful violin melody with gentle acoustic guitar strumming, celestial bells, and a warm, resolving piano theme
music_intent.mood_arc: Direct Camera → The Viking Shield → Stolen Cheese → Alaska Wind

scenes.setting: Present Day - Lakeside Pier (Exterior) - A Piece of Eternity / End Credits
scenes.story_day: Present Day - Lakeside Pier (Exterior) - A Piece of Eternity / End Credits
scenes.summary: Present Day - Lakeside Pier (Exterior) - A Piece of Eternity / End Credits — EPILOGUE: It was sunrise on Lake Michigan. I walked out to the end of the wooden pier, my heavy black sweater shielding me from the cool morning breeze.

Don’t you remember your responsibilities around here? Now we have to wait two more weeks 
because they can’t fit her in. What were you doing yesterday that was so important anyway?” 
His lips were in a tight line, and his face was getting red. I could see him clenching his fists, 
moving them in and out. 
--- PAGE 23 ---
 “I totally forgot. I’m sorry, I was with someone” I said. 
 “With someone? What the hell is that? With who?” Nick was trying not to lose it 
completely, and Ma just sat in her chair looking at us like nothing was up. 
 “I met a girl, and we had a couple dates. That’s all.” 
 Nick just stared at me with those black eyes and fire coming out the top of his head. “Oh, 
great. A girl. That’s worth all this. Don’t you think Ma deserves better than that? Try looking at 
your priorities Bro, you just might learn something.” 
 “I said I was sorry!” I was trying to explain, but he just turned around and walked out, 
slamming the door in my face. 
 That’s all I need. Nick mad at me. What a mess this was. I would have to talk to him 
soon, because I didn’t know when I would have to get Ma to the doctor now. I thought maybe 
tomorrow or the next day I would call him and see if he would even speak to me. I hardly ever 
get into it with Nick. I just don’t like arguing with people – especially Nick. He’s responsible 
and everything, but you just don’t know what he is going to do. 
 One time, back when he was in high school, I remember him getting so pissed off at this 
one kid named Steve. I think Steve said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend, and she came to 
him crying about it. Nick was so mad. He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the 
night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that 
crap on fire! I couldn’t believe it
--- PAGE 24 ---
herself together so well. He wouldn’t even understand her, and she definitely might be afraid of 
him, I know I would if I was a girl. 
 In my mind I was trying to figure out how I could make all the people in my life fit 
together. Ma, Nick, Sionna; they are all so different. Each one needs some special thing, some 
special care. Ma needs me. She needs me to take her places, make sure she gets medicine and her 
pills. She can’t even be trusted to sleep alone. Nick needs me because he needs me to take care 
of Ma. Sionna needs me. Sionna needs me? Where did I get that one? Maybe I was just hoping 
she needs me, without really knowing. It’s true she tells me stuff that makes me feel that way. 
She says I give her “insight”. Not sure how I do that. The other day we were talking about trees 
and how they change color in the fall. She told me the sugars get caught in the leaves. It’s like 
the trees are shutting down for the winter. The sugars get caught in the leaves and it makes them 
turn spectacular colors.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the quiet lakeside wooden pier at sunrise. holds the old photo of little Nick and P standing proudly beside a wooden fence in England, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: I looked at Nick's protective arm, his proud chest, and his brawler's grin. I didn't feel any pain or regret. I just felt a deep, warm love.

story_beats.visual_event: Character_P (38 years old, thin frame, black sweater, blue eyes) in the quiet lakeside wooden pier at sunrise. gently strokes the protective image of little Nick's arm around P's shoulders, smiling warmly, lips closed, internal monologue not spoken to another person

audio.speaker: Character_P
audio.dialogue: Thank you, Nick, I whispered to the water. Thank you for being my shield. I'll make sure they remember us. I'll make sure they know.

audio.speaker: Character_P

audio.speaker: Character_P

audio.speaker: Character_P
